title: Leveraging Generative AI for Security Defense of Mobile Edge-Enabled Digital Twins: A Survey
authors: Yaoqi Yang, Geng Sun, Hongyang Du, Changyuan Zhao, Zehui Xiong, Dusit Niyato, Abbas Jamalipour, Zhu Han, Khaled B. Letaief
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Addressing rising security threats that hinder the deployment of mobile edge-enabled digital twins (MDTs), this paper investigates the transformative potential of generative artificial intelligence (GAI) for enhancing MDT security frameworks. We propose a comprehensive five-layer MDT architecture and analyze various malicious security threats across physical and digital twin spaces. Focusing on GAI’s unique capabilities in data quality and diversity, we explore the application of variational autoencoders, generative adversarial networks, generated diffusion models, and transformer-based models within different layers of the MDT architecture. We also provide a summary of performance metrics, evaluation methodologies, and implementation considerations, concluding with key future research directions aimed at realizing real-time and complex attack defense strategies. Our findings show that GAI can significantly improve security measures for MDT systems, effectively addressing critical challenges in this rapidly evolving field.

title: Attribute–Topology Cross-Frequency Aligned Graph Neural Networks for Homophilic and Heterophilic Graphs in Node Classification
authors: Yachao Yang, Yanfeng Sun, Jipeng Guo, Jinlu Wang, Shaofan Wang, Junbin Gao, Fujiao Ju, Baocai Yin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Graph neural networks (GNNs) have excelled in handling graph-structured data, attracting significant research interest. However, two primary challenges have emerged: interference between topology and attributes distorting node representations, and the low-pass filtering nature of most GNNs leading to the oversight of valuable high-frequency information in graph signals. These issues are particularly pronounced in heterophilic graphs. To address these challenges, we propose attribute–topology cross-frequency aligned (ATCFA) GNNs. ATCFA combines low- and high-pass filters to capture both smooth and detailed representations from topological and attribute perspectives. It also enforces frequency-specific constraints to reduce noise and redundancy in each frequency band. The model can dynamically adjust the filtering ratios for both homophilic and heterophilic graphs. Crucially, ATCFA establishes dynamic associations between corresponding frequency components of topology and attribute, achieving systematic alignment and interactive fusion that explicitly mitigates interference and promotes complementary information utilization across domains. Extensive experiments on standard datasets show that ATCFA delivers higher classification accuracy than state-of-the-art methods, proving its capability to handle both homophilic and heterophilic graphs in node classification.

title: DMS: Throughput-Aware Distributed Multimodal Model Surgery on Embedded Devices
authors: Qixuan Cai, Xiulong Liu, Kaixuan Zhang, Xin Xie, Xinyu Tong, Keqiu Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) generates diverse sensory data, and aggregating this information can significantly improve the inference accuracy and robustness of deep learning models. As multimodal data often originates from distributed devices, efficiently deploying multimodal models on real devices with limited bandwidth has become a key topic. Though existing methods reduce the transmission delay by compressing the original data or segmenting the model, they ignore the heterogeneity of transmission throughput of distributed devices and the limited computing power of multiple embedded devices. This paper proposes <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">D</u>istributed <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">M</u>ultimodal model <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</u>urgery (DMS), the first model partitioning method for multimodal models on multiple distributed embedded devices. First, we propose a lightweight autoencoder with a tunable structure and a joint training method to compress the transmitted data; Second, we formulate the multimodal model partition as an optimization problem and propose an efficient model partitioning algorithm; Third, we build and test a real-world prototype system using three different datasets and models. The evaluation results for activity recognition indicate that DMS can reduce the inference delay by up to 58.33% while achieving a 0.22% higher accuracy than the best baseline method.

title: The Safety Frontier in LLMs: Risks, Defenses, and Open Challenges
authors: Ishika Goyal, Siva Sai, Aahana Gupta, Vinay Chamola
publish: IEEE Communications Standards Magazine
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT, Claude, and LLaMA are rapidly transforming domains including healthcare, education, law, and finance. However, their increasing adoption in high-stakes applications raises serious safety concerns. Without sufficient safeguards, LLM-powered AI systems are vulnerable to threats such as prompt injection, training data poisoning, model theft, denial-of-service through prompt abuse, misinformation generation, adversarial examples, model inversion, data leakage, and insider misuse. These vulnerabilities can produce harmful, biased, or misleading outputs, compromise privacy, and undermine trust. To address these risks, researchers have proposed safety approaches that aim to constrain and guide model behaviour, proactively detect and resist attacks, and incorporate ethical alignment into model design. Together, these methods form a multi-layered defence that combines preventive, detective, and corrective measures to improve LLM robustness and trustworthiness. This paper presents a comprehensive review of key safety mechanisms for LLMs, categorizing them into guardrails, red teaming, adversarial training, alignment techniques, jailbreak detection and prevention, watermarking, and fuzzing. For each category, recent advances are summarized, strengths and limitations are assessed, and open challenges are identified to support safer and more reliable real-world deployment of LLMs.

title: Robust Beamforming for RSMA-Aided Bistatic Integrated Sensing and Communication in Low-Altitude Wireless Networks
authors: Zhijie Lyu, Xuantao Lyu, Ye Wang, Rui Wang, Yi Gong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: The integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) technology enhances the next generation of wireless networks by introducing a new approach to secure communication. In this work, a novel rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA)-aided bistatic ISAC system is proposed to serve multiple users and to estimate the parameters of a moving target. The design of the beamforming matrix aims to both maximize weighted sum-rate (WSR) and minimize the Cramér-Rao bound (CRB) for the target under a power budget constraint. In particular, we consider a scenario where a dual-functional radar communication (DFRC) waveform is emitted with a trade-off between WSR for multiple users and CRB for a target. Inspired by the robust downlink communication capabilities of RSMA, users exhibit improved performance compared to the space division multiple access based ISAC, as it effectively addresses multi-user interference by decoding it partially and treating the remaining interference as noise. In order to strike a balance between communication and sensing requirements, the system combines two metrics of DFRC through a coefficient. Additionally, methods such as weighted minimum mean square error, semidefinite relaxation, and successive convex approximation are employed to tackle the issue of non-convexity. The simulation results demonstrate the performance gain and effectiveness of the proposed architecture over the baseline scheme.

title: Chirality and Handedness of the SKALA4.1 Antenna and Intra-Station Mitigation
authors: Tobia D. Carozzi, Paola Di Ninni, Mirko Bercigli
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: We discuss the chirality of the antenna used in the SKA-low radio telescope, called SKALA4.1. We find that it more readily accepts one circular handed radiation more than the other even along its boresight, i.e. zenith. This is in spite of the fact that vertical axis may appear at first sight to be a symmetry axis. But in fact, the mechanical structure of the SKALA4.1 antenna is not mirror symmetric, so it is a chiral antenna. This can explain its handedness preference. This can lead to a nonzero instrumental Stokes circular polarization component, also known as instrumental Stokes V. The bias in Stokes V can be difficult to calibrate and may make certain science cases more difficult. As a potential part of a mitigation strategy, we investigate the possibility of also using the specular (mirror-image) version of SKALA4.1, since the specular SKALA4.1 is found to have the exact opposite handedness of the nominal SKALA4.1. We find that mixing nominal and specular antennas does indeed decrease the beamformed bias in handedness.

title: Modeling Astrocyte-Driven Repair of Visuomotor Deficits in Alzheimer's Thalamic Circuitry_supp1-3682803.pdf
authors: Amit Konar
publish: N/A
abstract: Alzheimer's Disease (AD) frequently manifests in visuomotor impairments, disrupting cholinergic signaling from the brainstem to the Thalamic Reticular Nucleus (TRN). The contribution of this work lies in the development of a biologically informed computational framework to model astrocyte mediated restoration of synaptic transmission, measured by a ‘release probability (PR)’ in TRN under cholinergic depletion. The model simulates three physiological states—healthy, damaged, and recovered by incorporating dual astrocytic mechanisms i.e., depolarization induced suppression excitation (DSE) and endocannabinoid mediated synaptic potentiation (e-SP). This is validated through in-vivo experiments conducted using functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) in mice, performing visuomotor integration tasks. Both computational simulations and experimental measurements demonstrate that astrocytic feedback from functional thalamocortical relay cells (TCR) and interneurons (IN) can restore PR partially to approximately 70% of healthy levels despite complete ACh depletion. The bounded, asymmetric temporal dynamics, consistent with beta distribution characteristics reflect biologically realistic regulatory mechanisms maintaining synaptic homeostasis, exhibiting rapid initial potentiation followed by gradual decay. Sensitivity analyses reveal that when modulated with identical parameter values, excitatory TCR terminals produce greater PR with increasing astrocytic strengthening rate of indirect signaling, while inhibitory IN terminals dominate PR recovery under elevated astrocyte synapse coupling weights of indirect signaling. This highlights how excitatory and inhibitory astrocyte-targeted pathways work in tandem to shape recovery in a coordinated manner. The quantitative agreement between computational predictions and experimental measurements (within 2% error) provides evidence supporting astrocyte-based neuromodulation as a biologically grounded mechanism for restoring visuomotor function in AD.

title: Global Trust: Lightweight, Event-Driven Trust Management for Blackhole-Resilient IoT Routing
authors: Bhaskar Maithani, Ajay Vikram Singh, Said Broumi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) enables critical services in healthcare, smart cities, and industry, but its cooperative and resource-constrained nature makes it vulnerable to routing-layer attacks. A blackhole attack-where a node advertises attractive routes and then drops all relayed traffic—can severely degrade delivery performance in protocols such as AODV and RPL. While cryptographic protections secure links, they cannot prevent insiders from misbehaving and are often too heavy for low-power devices. To address this, we propose Global Trust, a lightweight trust-management layer that computes a Global Trust (GT) score from Direct Trust (DT), Indirect Trust (IT), and Historical Trust (HT), and filters low-GT nodes during route formation. Unlike prior heavy pipelines (fuzzy logic, blockchain, ML), our approach is event-driven—recomputing trust only on topology changes, new route requests, or a periodic refresh-to bound overhead. We implement and evaluate Global Trust in MATLAB specifically under blackhole conditions using practical metrics-Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR), Packet Drop Ratio, End-to-End Delay, and routing overhead. Results show that Global Trust sustains higher delivery and lower packet drops than AODV while keeping delay stable, with only modest overhead. This demonstrates that simple, reproducible trust models, when combined with event-driven execution, can significantly harden IoT routing against blackhole attacks without imposing heavy computational cost.

title: Object Distance Monitoring in Crisis Scenarios Using Deep Learning Based Yolo-7 Model
authors: I. Vasudevan, M. Mohamed Nasurudeen, S. Siddik, M. Vasudevan, M. Senthil Kumar, R. M. Dilip Charaan
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: In terms of public health emergencies, to improve health safety and to minimize contagious/airborne disease spread advancement, much of the world adopted a minimum two-meter physical distancing rule in order to facilitate self-care of the individual. This research presents existing distancemonitoring technologies and tools and applications in crisis situations, with the focus on deploying the deep learning techniques to YOLO-7 based distance management to reduce disease spread. The application of AI-based systems to automatically detect individuals and alert persons to a two-meter detected physical distance are the main focus of this study. The AI-based systems in this application rely largely on existing public camera networks. AI-based systems are also used to track time spent and monitor location for individuals in terms of person-to-person detection. The deep learning-based YOLO-7 model is improved the feature extraction for high resolution object with missed detection in crisis scenarios and also achieved more accuracy.

title: Comparative Analysis of Circular and Dual-Band Diode-Based Reconfigurable Circular Patch Antennas: Design and Performance
authors: Harjas Kaur, Allu Deekshita, Mahankali Mahith, Koppisetti Bharath Kumar, Smrity Dwivedi
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: This paper describes a design procedure that begins with a traditional circular patch antenna and upgrades it to a reconfigurable design and then analyses the use of superstrate layers for increasing the gain, which originally was a major problem with reconfigurable antennas. The proposed design starts with architecture which was carefully defined as a central circular slot and then made reconfigurable by placing a varactor diode across the slot. Additionally, a superstrate layer is added to increase the gain significantly. The antenna was thus analysed in terms of bandwidth, gain, and radiation patterns and then modified to a reconfigurable design, where the varactor diode integrated into the design allowed for real-time frequency reconfigurability. This major breakthrough essentially shows the need for multiple narrowband antennas, as a single antenna can now operate in multiple frequency bands. A comparative analysis shows that the reconfigurable design offers major improvements in terms of flexibility and gain with consistent radiation efficiency and patterns.

title: Parametric Study of the Insertion of DGS Type Structure with Geometry Based on SETR Type Metamaterial Applied in Rectangular Patch Microstrip Antennas
authors: Arthur Mendes da Silva, Elison Wediley dos Reis Silva, Caiky Breno Santiago Chaves, Pedro Arthur Freitas da Silva, Alan Pedro Vasconcelos Martins, Humberto Dionísio de Andrade
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: This work aims to do a parametric analysis of an antenna initially designed to operate in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S$</tex> band, between 2 and 4 GHz, with the insertion of SETR (Split Equilateral Triangle Resonator) metamaterial structures in the ground plane to form a DGSs (Defected Ground Structure), in order to verify the influence of these changes on the antenna radiation parameters. The purpose is to make comparisons between the modified antennas and a reference antenna. Changes in the resonance frequency, bandwidth and reflection coefficient of the antennas were demonstrated.

title: Parametric Study of a Circularly Patch DGS Antenna with SETR Inspired Metamaterial Structures
authors: Arthur Mendes da Silva, Elison Wediley dos Reis Silva, Caiky Breno Santiago Chaves, Pedro Arthur Freitas da Silva, Alan Pedro Vasconcelos Martins, Humberto Dionísio de Andrade
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: The objective of this study is to parametrically analyze the insertion of SETR (Split Equilateral Ring Resonator) structures in microstrip antennas with a circular patch in order to form DGS (Defected Ground Structures). The analysis of the radiation parameters of the simulated and fabricated variant antennas showed shifts in the resonance frequency to the left of the reference, as well as changes in bandwidth and reflection coefficient.

title: A Federated Learning Approach to Alleviate Data Heterogeneity in Smart Grid
authors: Ming Lei, Jiajie Sun, Yeshen He, Yiying Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: The rapid development of smart grids has integrated various distributed energy resources and monitoring devices, producing large volumes of heterogeneous, non-IID data. This data diversity challenges traditional centralized machine learning, while federated learning (FL), a decentralized approach, struggles with convergence instability in non-IID settings within cyber-physical systems like smart grids. To address these issues, we propose FedCSG, a novel federated learning framework tailored for distributed power systems. FedCSG employs a hierarchical communication structure with clients, sub-servers, and a central server to reduce communication costs and improve scalability. It uses cluster-based model aggregation, grouping clients by data distribution similarity via Jensen-Shannon divergence, followed by bias-weighted parameter fusion to counter client drift. Additionally, an adaptive Dropout mechanism adjusts dropout rates based on neuron activation and device performance, enhancing training efficiency across diverse resources. Evaluations on benchmark datasets (MNIST, SVHN) and the IEEE 118-Bus power system dataset show FedCSG outperforms baseline methods, achieving 93.12% accuracy and a 91.34% F1 score on the IEEE dataset, effectively addressing data heterogeneity in power systems.

title: Nanomaterial Synthesis by Dense Medium Glow Discharge: Improvement of Efficiency by Design of Experiment Model
authors: Sinem Sevim Talay Ozdemir, Yaprak Yalçin, Dilek Cokeliler Serdaroglu, F. Belgin Atac, Kumru Didem Atalay, Ismail Cengiz Kocum, Hande Pamukcu, Berna Dengiz
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Biomedical Engineering and Technology (ICBET)
abstract: Orthodontic retention is essential for maintaining teeth in their corrected positions after active orthodontic treatment; however, retention appliances such as the Hawley retainer often promote biofilm formation, caries, and gingival inflammation. To address these complications, this study introduces carbon-based (C-C) and carbon-nitrogen (C-N) nanoparticles synthesized via dense medium plasma technology into polymethylmethacrylate (PMMA) dental composites. Full factorial experimental design (2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{C}, 2^{4}$</tex> for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{N}$</tex>) was applied to optimize nanoparticle synthesis parameters, including power, electrode distance, argon flow, and polarity (for C-N). Efficiency was evaluated by yield (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mg} / \mathrm{L}$</tex>), and cytocompatibility was assessed via MTT assays on periodontal ligament cells. C-N nanoparticles demonstrated higher production yield and sustained biocompatibility across multiple concentrations, while C-C nanoparticles showed a biphasic viability response, with reduced effects at higher doses. Statistical analysis (via MINITAB 21) confirmed significant main and interaction effects, particularly for argon flow and polarity in C-N synthesis. These findings suggest that C-N-doped PMMA composites may offer a promising antimicrobial and biocompatible solution for orthodontic retention applications.

title: Fuzzy Cascade PID Control of Core Outlet Temperature Based on Novel Gas-Cooled Microreactors
authors: Yongkang Xing, Lin Guo, Shengyong Liao, Changhao Wu, Mingyue Jiang, Ying Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: The novel gas-cooled micro-reactor exhibits strong non-linear dynamic characteristics and multi-variable coupling, as well as complex thermal-hydraulic coupling features, thus imposing higher demands on control technologies. Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) control is inadequate for adapting to a wide range of varying operating conditions, and fixed-parameter controllers are unable to cope with complex operational scenarios. To address these requirements, a fuzzy control (FC) algorithm has been introduced based on a cascade PID controller for the core outlet temperature, resulting in the proposal of a fuzzy cascade PID control scheme that allows for online tuning of PID parameters. A fuzzy control system model was established using Simulink, and simulations were conducted to validate the designed control system under two scenarios: step changes in load and linear variations. The results indicate that the proposed fuzzy controller can achieve superior control performance across various operating conditions, with the Integral of Time-weighted Squared Error (ITSE) serving as the control metric demonstrating an order of magnitude improvement in certain scenarios.

title: A Wideband Magneto-Electric Dipole Based Slant Polarized Antenna with Metasurface
authors: Md. Imran Khan, Antonella Maria Loconsole, Francesco Anelli, Vito Vincenzo Francione, Francesco Prudenzano
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: A wideband metasurface (MS) based slant (+45°) polarized magneto-electric (ME) dipole antenna is proposed for 5G millimeter wave applications. The integration of MS with ME dipole allows to achieve a simulated impedance bandwidth BW = 6 GHz (24-30 GHz). The simulated fractional 2 dB realized gain bandwidth is BW<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</inf> = 22.2% (24-30 GHz) and the simulated maximum realized gain is RG<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">max</inf> = 8.2 dB at the frequency f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf> = 29 GHz, with a simulated radiation efficiency over the impedance bandwidth $\eta\gt 0.94$. The above features make this antenna suitable for 5G millimeter wave (mm-wave) applications.

title: Evaluating the Impact of AI on Customer Service Efficiency and Customer Satisfaction: A Management Perspective
authors: Namrata Pancholi, Anshika Tiwari, Mona Chaudhary, Mauktik Kalra, Ira Jain
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: In today's dynamic business environment, organizations are increasingly integrating artificial intelligence (AI) solutions to enhance service quality and sustain competitive advantage. Among these innovations, AI-powered chatbots have gained prominence as tools for transforming customer service operations and banking is one such early adopters of the technology using AI for increasing customer services and efficiency. The research employs a descriptive design, relying on primary data obtained via structured questionnaires administered to customers who have used AI chatbots for routine service interactions. Supplementary secondary data from scholarly publications and industry reports enhance the conceptual foundation and managerial analysis. Results indicate that AI chatbots substantially improve customer service performance by minimizing response time, ensuring round-the-clock accessibility, and alleviating employee workload. Satisfaction levels remain high for straightforward and repetitive inquiries; however, chatbot performance declines when addressing complex or emotionally sensitive issues. The study concludes that adopting a hybrid service architecture-integrating automated chatbot interactions with personalized human assistance-can optimize both efficiency and user experience.

title: The Evolution of Stethoscopes and the Potential of Smart Stethoscopes as Future Technology: A Systematic Review of Cardiac and Pulmonary Diagnostics
authors: Mohammad Rizqi Abdul Basyith, Hartanto Prawibowo, Mochammad Ariyanto, Rifky Ismail
publish: 2025 IEEE International Biomedical Instrumentation and Technology Conference (IBITeC)
abstract: The stethoscope has long served as a cornerstone in medical diagnostics, yet conventional auscultation remains heavily dependent on clinical expertise and is susceptible to environmental noise interference. With advancements in technology, wireless digital stethoscopes offer improved flexibility and sound amplification. However, challenges such as surrounding noise interference and the inability to distinguish between heart and lung sounds continue to hinder diagnostic optimization. This systematic review analyzes various innovations in wireless and smart stethoscopes, with a focus on the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) and noise-cancelling systems. Based on this analysis, the article also introduces the development roadmap of CBIOM3S, a wireless digital stethoscope designed for real-time, AI-based cardiopulmonary diagnosis. The development plan includes the integration of a CNN-LSTM hybrid model for automated sound classification, the application of adaptive noise filtering within the AI pipeline, and the intelligent separation of heart and lung sounds prior to disease identification. This approach aims to transform the wireless digital stethoscope CBIOM3S from a conventional wireless device into an intelligent diagnostic tool that is faster, more reliable, and context-aware, supporting modern clinical needs as well as telemedicine applications.

title: Dual-Polarized Phased Subarray at Millimeter-Wave Band with 6 Beamformer Chips and 48 Elements
authors: Ryotaro Ohashi, Hideki Ueda, Kengo Onaka
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: For the implementation of millimeter-wave wireless communication, antenna modules that assembled an antenna array and RF chips on a substrate board are essential. Antenna modules for base stations and access points have been developed with multiple beamformer (BF) chips to meet the requirements of high antenna gain, high EIRP and beamforming. In this paper, a dual-polarized antenna module with 6 BF chips is designed by using a 1-input and 6-output Wilkinson network that consist of equal and unequal dividers. The antenna module is composed of a phased subarray with a triangular grid of 2-element subarrays to improve the antenna gain at the boresight and suppress the grating lobes. As measurement results, the antenna gain is 23.0 dBi and the beam scanning range is ±50° in the horizontal plane and ±20° in the vertical plane.

title:
Biodegradable Chitosan-Nb
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>5</sub>
                    Substrates for Microwave Devices
authors: Mirelli S. da Silva, Samanta M. de Holanda, Leonardo O. de Sousa, Francisco de A. Brito Filho, Isaac B. T. da Silva
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: This paper present different biodegradable substrate proposals based on chitosan biopolymer. The chitosan thin films were prepared using a 2% acetic acid solution and 2% chitosan dry mass. Dielectric properties were enhanced by niobium pentoxide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{Nb}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{5}\right)$</tex>, with concentrations proposals: 1%, 2.5%. 5.0% and 10%. The dielectric characterizations presented large reduction in loss tangent with the insertion of only 1% of Nb<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</inf>, maintaining permittivity almost unaltered. Permittivity could also be increased as Nb<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</inf> percentage increased, keeping the losses at low values. Two configurations of radial stubs were proposed and simulated at HFSS using the dielectric characterizations as input. The results showed that only 1% of Nb<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</inf> led to a great performance in the stopband and passband in both configurations of the filter.

title: Antennas Optimization Techniques for Integration of Wireless Applications using Angle-of-Arrival
authors: Walid El Hajj, Nawfal Asrih, Fabien Ferrero
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: In this paper, we present some design optimization techniques ensuring robust integration of antennas in wireless devices where the Phase Difference of Arrival (PDoA) at multiple antenna(s) output are measured vs. the Angle-of-Arrival (AoA) of incident EM wave for localization purposes. Those techniques can be applied to Ultra-Wide Band technologies or any other wireless technology supporting such features. The reliability of those techniques e.g. mirroring, adding parasitic elements, are demonstrated using a specific antenna design by adding/removing the corresponding design elements and showing that performance is independent/dependent on integration aspects e.g. device chassis, environment, etc.

title: Gene Expression Dynamics in Endometriosis and Hashimoto Disease: Insights into Potential Overlaps
authors: Ruojue Wang, Beibei Hu, Yuxuan Du, Manlin Zhang
publish: 2025 15th International Conference on Biomedical Engineering and Technology (ICBET)
abstract: Objective: Investigate the relationship between endometriosis and Hashimoto's thyroiditis and analyze their potential molecular mechanisms. Methods: Employed multiple analytical methods including Mendelian Randomization Analysis using GWAS data, mRNA expression analysis from GEO database, GeneFriends analysis, and gene regulatory network construction. Results: No direct causal relationship found. mRNA expression analysis revealed common upregulated and downregulated genes associated with cell structure/function and phosphorylation/deamination. NOTCH signaling pathway was significantly enriched. GeneFriends analysis identified TNFRSF21 and MBNL3 as highly expressed genes. Regulatory network analysis identified TFs and miRNAs potentially regulating these genes. Conclusion: No direct causal relationship, but significant overlap in mRNA expression, pathways, and immune regulation suggests shared molecular mechanisms. Future research can explore shared therapeutic targets.

title: Bearing Fault Recognition Method Integrating Multi-Scale Features and Attention Mechanism
authors: Yilin Yang, Yiqian Jia, Ruoqing Wu, Sumin Guo
publish: 2025 8th World Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Intelligent Manufacturing (WCMEIM)
abstract: The accurate and early recognition of bearing faults is critical for the predictive maintenance of rotating machinery. While deep learning models, particularly Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), have shown promise in this domain, they often struggle to capture both local detailed features and global contextual information from vibration signals effectively. This paper proposes a novel bearing fault recognition method that integrates multi-scale feature extraction with a channel-spatial attention mechanism. The core of our approach is a dedicated neural network architecture designed to process raw vibration data. It first employs parallel convolutional layers with different kernel sizes to extract rich, multi-scale features. Subsequently, an attention module is incorporated to adaptively weight these features, enhancing the discriminative characteristics crucial for fault classification and suppressing less informative ones. Experiments were conducted on the publicly available Case Western Reserve University (CWRU) bearing dataset. Results demonstrate that our method achieves a recognition accuracy of 99.8%, outperforming standard CNN and other baseline models. Further analysis under noisy conditions (Signal-to-Noise Ratio of 0 dB) shows a robustness improvement, with accuracy maintained above 97.5%, highlighting its superiority in real-world industrial environments. This work provides a validated and effective framework for intelligent fault diagnosis.

title: Predictive Analytics for Crime Prevention: Ethical, Legal and Technical Challenges
authors: Abhinav Gupta, Kshitij Jain, Farkhod Mardanov, Sandjar Dusmanov, Ametova Aysulu Mnajatdinovna, Abdukosim Xaitov
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Predictive analytics has become an effective crime prevention tool as it allows law enforcement agencies to predict the future occurrence of a crime and invest resources before the fact; but its use presents a lot of theoretical, legal, and technical issues of bias, transparency, privacy, accountability. The paper outlines an ethical-legal-technical system of predictive crime analytics that encompasses data governance, biased modeling, explainable machine learning, compliance assessment, and decision-making by a human being into the predictive system. Empirical findings prove that the proposed methodology has a competitive predictive performance and a significant reduction of algorithm bias, an improvement in the interpretability and an increase in legal and ethical standards compared to traditional models. The results suggest the possibility of ensuring the effectiveness of crime prevention in a way that does not fully undermine the basic rights, democratic principles, and the confidence of the community and society, by means of responsible, governance-conscious predictive analytics.

title: Influencer or Finfluencer: A Comprehensive Study of Behavioral Finance in the Age of Digital Social Media
authors: Pooja Devi, Sucharita Sharma, Priyanka Agarwal, Rakesh Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Over the years, usage of social media has increased which showcases one of the critical changes in the way investors make decisions, thus radically affecting behavioural finance. This research has given a try to figure out the influence that the online platforms have on investor behaviour and taking into consideration, the way social media increases cognitive biases and affects herd behaviour. Social media platforms like Twitter, Reddit and YouTube have emerged as source of real-time information and market sentiments. Opinions and trends have emerged rapidly changing investor decisions. This research henceforth studies and figures out the psychological influence of social media exposure in relation with market fluctuations and speculative activities via the lenses of FOMO (fear of missing out), presumptions and trading on emotion. High-profile events as the GameStop short squeeze prove with the help of examples that how social media-driven chronicles seep into the traditional financial analysis and cause major divergence in the formation of the price of assets from their inherent values. It orates regulatory issues alongside raising the significance of investor education as a kind of counterbalance to the not been verified information and spontaneous behaviour connected with trading. Therefore, conclusions emerging from this research find that social media platforms enlarge the courses of action to access financial knowledge, investors will remain to be compelled to embrace a more informed and risk-averse strategy since online platforms carry on to be dynamic. Consequently, understanding innuendos on behavioural finance has to be essential to notify adaptable investment strategies.

title: Assessment of Osteonecrosis-Induced Changes in the Viscoelastic and Biomechanical Properties of Human Trabecular Bone
authors: Rajesh Kumar Bhushan, Ajeesh M Kurup, Navin Kumar, Naveen Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Biomedical Instrumentation and Technology Conference (IBITeC)
abstract: Osteonecrosis is a degenerative condition that disrupts bone's structural integrity and raises the likelihood of fragility-related fractures. Because bone is viscoelastic, its mechanical performance varies with the applied loading rate. This investigation examines how osteonecrosis influences the viscoelastic response of human trabecular bone and explores its relationship to microstructural and mechanical features. Trabecular bone samples were collected from femoral heads of 18 patients with osteonecrosis, all with hip fragility fractures. From each femur head, both the necrotic and healthy regions were sampled for analysis. Micro-computed tomography (micro-CT) was used to assess bone architecture. Dynamic mechanical analysis (DMA) and Stress relaxation tests were conducted to characterize viscoelastic behavior. Statistical correlation was applied to determine links between viscoelasticity, microstructural indices, and biomechanical parameters. Osteo-necrotic bone exhibited markedly different stress relaxation and frequency sweep profiles compared to healthy bone. Reduced storage modulus, initial stress, and initial stiffness were evident in the diseased group. Stress relaxation percentage correlated inversely with mineral content (r = −0.52, p = 0.003) and mineral-to-matrix ratio (r = −0.43, p = 0.009), and directly with organic content (r = 0.40, p = 0.02). Storage and loss modulus values also showed significant associations with volume fraction of bone (BV/TV) across both regions. The findings indicate that osteonecrosis alters the time-dependent mechanical performance of trabecular bone (femoral). Higher organic content appears beneficial for viscoelastic function, whereas excessive mineralization reduces it. These results highlight the role of compositional changes in weakening osteonecrotic bone and increasing fracture susceptibility.

title: Aerial Image Analysis System Captured with UAV for Early Detection of Maize Foliar Diseases Using Lightweight YOLO Variants
authors: Pablo Sari, Hugo Torres
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: The use of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) has enhanced the development of remote sensing systems for agricultural monitoring. This study presents a deep learning-based system for the early detection of foliar diseases in maize using aerial images captured with a drone. Three lightweight YOLO variants are employed: YOLOv8n, YOLOv8s, and YOLOv9c, selected for their efficiency and feasibility in embedded computing environments. The methodological workflow includes aerial acquisition, manual annotation, preprocessing, and training. In addition, aspects associated with the propagation of electromagnetic radiation in the visible range are considered, including acquisition geometry, footprint, leaf reflectance, and spectral variability. The qualitative results show that the system makes it possible to identify anomalous patterns on the leaves, demonstrating its potential for early monitoring in real environments.

title: The Open-Source Model for Ryodoraku Acupoint Conductance Measurement
authors: Hoang Linh Le Thi, Ja Lam Nguyen, Hai Anh Nguyen Thi, Anh Tu Tran, Huong Duong Nguyen Thi, Ngoc Dang Tran
publish: 2025 IEEE International Biomedical Instrumentation and Technology Conference (IBITeC)
abstract: This study presents a low-cost, open-source device designed for Ryodoraku acupoint conductance measurement. The device applies a low-level stimulation current (2.17 mA) and converts skin conductance signals into a normalized energy scale ranging from 0 to 200. Validation experiments were performed on a healthy 20 -year-old female volunteer. Under stable resistance conditions from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$18 \mathrm{k} \Omega$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \mathrm{M} \Omega$</tex>, the device showed excellent agreement with the commercial reference system (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=0.99, R^{2}=0.98$</tex>, RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=6.62$</tex>). Repeatability testing with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 1}$</tex> consecutive measurements at Ryodoraku points revealed only minor fluctuations (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<10$</tex> units), indicating stable performance. In vivo assessments at designated acupoints further demonstrated strong consistency with the reference system (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=0.93, \mathbf{R}^{2}=0.86$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R M S E}=9.42$</tex>). These findings confirm the technical robustness of the device and support its potential utility as a practical tool for organ function evaluation based on Ryodoraku measurements.

title: Enhanced Conditional Diffusion Models for 2D Medical Image Augmentation in Tumor Detection
authors: P Revathi, R Vanmathi, V Indulakshmi, P Saranya Devi, K Abrar Ahmed
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Detecting and segmenting brain tumors is essential for accurate diagnosis and effective treatment planning. Yet deep learning models often fall short because annotated medical datasets are limited, making it difficult for models to generalize across different tumor types and imaging modalities. To overcome this challenge, we introduce a diffusion-driven framework that generates high-quality synthetic brain tumor MRI scans and integrates them with segmentation models to improve both accuracy and robustness. The proposed approach uses a Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model (DDPM) to capture the complex structure of MRI data and synthesize realistic tumor images from pure noise. Unlike GAN-based techniques, diffusion models train more reliably and avoid issues such as mode collapse, while still preserving fine anatomical and textural details. These synthetic scans are combined with real MRI data to create a richer training set, which helps strengthen segmentation performance, particularly in low-data settings. A U-Net-based segmentation network is then trained on this expanded dataset to precisely identify tumor regions. The system is evaluated using metrics such as Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC), Intersection over Union (IoU), Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM). Experimental results demonstrate notable gains in segmentation stability and image quality. Overall, this diffusion-supported pipeline offers a practical way to mitigate data scarcity by uniting synthetic image generation with supervised segmentation. The work also provides a strong foundation for future advancements in areas such as 3D volumetric MRI synthesis, multimodal tumor analysis, and deployment in real clinical workflows.

title: Beyond Symmetry: A Novel Asymmetric Interaction Strategy for Immersive VR-Based Mirror Therapy
authors: Tian Zhou, Lin Chen, Xiaocheng Wang, Xiao Fang, Xin Lu, Runjie Ni, Shu Chen, Zerong Wei, Rong Lau, Yanmei Yu, XiangMing Ye, Dongrong Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Abstract-Virtual Reality (VR) has been widely applied in healthcare domain. Virtual Reality Mirror Therapy (VRMT) is one such clinical application for phantom limb pain management and post-stroke hemiparesis rehabilitation. However, the prevailing design of VRMT tasks typically follows the schema of traditional mirror therapy paradigm that prescribes strictly symmetric bilateral movements along the body's midline. While symmetry is not a requirement by the mirror neuron therapy where mirror therapies derive, this constraint imposes unnecessary limitations to both the development of diversified game-based rehabilitation tasks and portability of VR equipment and hardware. As a result, average patients using a conventional VR system may often find it difficult to perform certain exercises, such as arm abductions, or not possible to run a VRMT paradigm on affordable VR equipment for home-based rehabilitation. We therefore propose AsymmVRMT, an asymmetric strategy for mirrored affected limb mapping, and adopted it in our VRMT paradigm. We conducted a randomized controlled trial (RCT) on post-stroke hemiparetic patients, and examined their clinical outcome measures (FM-UE, BI & WMFT) along with their user experience data (SUS & VEQ). Results showed that patients demonstrated stronger adaptability and more positive perceptions toward using the AsymmVRMT model, while the therapeutic outcomes were as good as those using conventional VRMT. AsymmVRMT therefore appears to be an effective alternative for human-computer interaction in VR that may offer higher flexibility and diversity to address the hardware-related constraints in rehabilitation system development, facilitating convenient VR-based rehabilitation for patients, both at the hospital and at home.

title: 10K is Enough: An Ultra-Lightweight Binarized Network for Infrared Small-Target Detection
authors: Biqiao Xin, Qianchen Mao, Bingshu Wang, Jiangbin Zheng, Yong Zhao, C.L. Philip Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The widespread deployment of Infrared Small-Target Detection (IRSTD) algorithms on edge devices necessitates the exploration of model compression techniques. Binarized neural networks (BNNs) are distinguished by their exceptional efficiency in model compression. However, the small size of infrared targets introduces stringent precision requirements for the IRSTD task, while the inherent precision loss during binarization presents a significant challenge. To address this, we propose a Binarized Infrared Small-Target Detection Network (BiisNet), which preserves the core operations of binarized convolutions while integrating full-precision features into the network’s information flow. Specifically, we propose a Dot Binary Convolution to retain fine-grained semantic information in feature maps while still leveraging the binarized convolution operations. In addition, we introduce a smooth and adaptive Dynamic Softsign function, which provides more comprehensive and progressively finer gradient during backpropagation, enhancing model stability and promoting an optimal weight distribution. Experimental results demonstrate that BiisNet achieves an mIoU of 68.23% with only 10k parameters, delivering an 11.56% improvement over the previous best binary neural network while remaining competitive with state-of-the-art full-precision models. The implementation code is available at https://github.com/joeyxin-del/BiisNet.

title: Communication Efficient Adaptive Model-Driven Quantum Federated Learning
authors: Dev Gurung, Shiva Raj Pokhrel
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: Training federated learning (FL) at scale suffers from severe communication and heterogeneity constraints. These challenges are amplified in quantum federated learning (QFL), especially under non-IID data. We propose a model-driven QFL (mdQFL) framework that addresses communication overhead, scalability, and client drift through adaptive clustering and representative aggregation. The framework enables structured personalization and efficient update compression across rounds. mdQFL is the first QFL approach to jointly analyze training efficiency, personalization, and test generalization under heterogeneous conditions. Experiments across multiple datasets and quantum platforms show 50% communication reduction while maintaining or improving accuracy over standard QFL base-lines. We provide convergence guarantees and communication complexity bounds to establish scalability and robustness.

title: Five-Fold Partitioned 30 × 30 MIMO Equalization Using Mode-Group Specific Interface Quantization in 15-Mode Fiber SDM Transmission
authors: Nicolas Braig-Christophersen, Carsten Schmidt-Langhorst, Robert Elschner, Giammarco Di Sciullo, Menno van den Hout, Ruben S. Luis, Pierre Sillard, Cristian Antonelli, Chigo M. Okonkwo, Hideaki Furukawa, Georg Rademacher, Colja Schubert, Ronald Freund
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: The increasing capacity demand of optical communication systems has driven research on space-division multiplexing (SDM) using few-mode fibers (FMFs). While FMFs provide high spatial efficiency, their practical deployment is limited by the rapidly growing complexity of multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) digital signal processing (DSP). Partitioned-MIMO architectures reduce DSP complexity by exploiting the existence of mode groups (MGs) in FMFs, where intra-group coupling is strong and inter-group coupling is weak. In this work, we experimentally investigate a partitioned MIMO architecture with mode-group specific interface quantization for a 15-mode FMF transmission system, which would otherwise require a full 30 × 30 MIMO equalizer. The equalizer is decomposed into five MG-specific sub-equalizers with low-resolution, MG-dependent inter-partition signal exchange to compensate residual inter-group coupling. Using 24.5-GBd dual polarization QPSK transmission over a 58.9-km graded-index FMF, we evaluate the impact of interface quantization on the achievable signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). The results show that weak inter-group coupling can be omitted entirely, while stronger neighboring MG interactions require only three to four bits to limit SNR penalties to below 1.7 dB relative to a uniform five-bit reference. This approach significantly reduces inter-partition interface throughput while preserving equalization performance, demonstrating the scalability of partitioned-MIMO architectures for high-capacity SDM systems.

title: An AI-Based Framework for Automated Fraud Risk Assessment in Auditing
authors: Navita Mahajan, Mahek Agarwal, Anushka Garg, Ahmad Yousuf Sulaiman, Adarsh V Raju
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The growing dependence on digital financial systems has significantly increased the complexity of fraud risk faced by organizations and auditors. Traditional audit approaches, which largely rely on manual procedures and periodic reviews, often struggle to detect emerging fraud risks in a timely manner. In recent years, the availability of large volumes of external data has created new opportunities to enhance fraud risk assessment through data-driven and automated techniques. This study proposes an AI-based framework for automated fraud risk assessment using largescale consumer complaint data. The analysis is based on a dataset exceeding 7.5 GB, containing consumer complaints related to financial institutions across multiple years. By applying exploratory data analysis and visual analytics, the study identifies key fraud risk indicators related to issue concentration, company-level exposure, response timeliness, temporal escalation, geographic clustering, and complaint lifecycle inefficiencies. The findings demonstrate that consumer complaint data can serve as an effective early warning signal of operational weaknesses and potential fraud risk. The proposed framework supports risk-based audit planning by enabling auditors to prioritize high-risk areas and enhance proactive fraud monitoring using transparent and interpretable analytical techniques.

title: Retinal Vessel Geometry as a Surrogate Marker Coronary Artery Calcification: A Deep Learning Approach
authors: Yazhini R, Dhiya J, Lokesh S M, Bhobesh Adhithiyan S G
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Coronary Artery Calcification (CAC) has been shown to be a strong predictor of cardiovascular disease (CVD). It is most commonly done using computed tomography (CT) which is expensive and exposes one to ionizing radiation. This study introduces a new non-invasive deep learning framework that employs the geometry of retinal vessels as a surrogate biomarker for CAC risk prediction. This work primarily presents a hybrid CNN-Vessel Graph Transformer (VGT) architecture that jointly models local vessel appearance and global vascular topology. The process involves the use of retinal fundus images, which were used as input for the STARE dataset. Further, the images are preprocessed to enhance as well as skeletonize the vessels. Subsequently, the graph is constructed to show the connectivity of the vessels. Alongside the deep digestible representations obtained from the CNN-VGT pipeline, we also combine clinically interpretable handcrafted vascular features such as tortuosity, branching angle, vessel density, fractal dimension etc. to enhance the robustness of the prediction. Using XGBoost classification of the fused feature representation resulted in an accuracy of 88 % with a ROC-AUC of 0.98 and high recall for the high-risk class, an important criterion for clinical screening. Supported by experimental results, the framework enables the characterization of both geometric and relational vascular patterns. The framework is found to be scalable, radiation-free and cost-effective for early stratification of the cardiovascular risk.

title: RedLife: Enhancing Accessibility and Efficiency in Blood Donation through Mobile Technology
authors: Shrishti Patel, Manshi Singh, Krishna Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The blood we donate is a lifeline in emergencies and for those who require long-term treatments. You can save up to three lives from a single pint of blood. For all the advancement, shortage of blood during medical crisis is still a major problem in most countries and leads to avoidable deaths. Current blood donation systems are scattered, slow and do not achieve real-time integration for donors, recipients and hospitals. This paper presents a Flutter Blood Donation Mobile Application which act as an interface to integrate drug donors, patients and blood banks in the same platform. The app includes features such as user donor registration, geo-location based blood search for the needy and realtime updates on the request to make sure that you receives timely blood. This system enables better healthcare response and is working towards the Sustainable Development Goal of Good Health.

title: Local Surface Approximation Contours for Virtual Reality Stylisation
authors: Amir Zaidi, Ricardo Marroquim, Michael Weinmann, Elmar Eisemann
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Line art is an illustrative technique with a wide use in education and art. In the context of image abstraction, its potential for increasing memorisation and recognition has been demonstrated, which motivates its use in scientific illustrations. While much work has focused on the conversion of 3D models into a line-art representation, there is a lack of solutions for virtual reality. Applying existing methods for each eye independently turns out to fall short due to cost constraints, distracting artifacts due to inconsistencies, or limitations regarding the input geometry. To address these limitations, we present a contour renderer for virtual reality. It operates in screen space, making it flexible, yet it relies on a local surface approximation combined with a registration error metric for robustness. Inconsistent occluding contours are continuously merged, and lines with no correspondence between both eyes are culled. The method is easy to implement, highly efficient even for high-resolution imagery, and, according to user evaluations, avoids the noticeable artifacts produced by existing work.

title: LIVE-GS: LLM Powers Interactive VR Experience with Physics-Aware Gaussian Splatting
authors: Haotian Mao, Hangyu Zhou, Zhuoxiong Xu, Siyue Wei, Yule Quan, Yan Zhang, Zixuan Guo, Nianchen Deng, Xubo Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: As 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) emerges as a leading approach for novel view synthesis and scene reconstruction, its potential in digital asset creation has gained significant attention. An increasing number of asset libraries based on GS are being established. However, generating physics-based dynamic assets remains a time-consuming and expertise-intensive task, especially for non-experts. In this paper, we propose LIVE-GS, a highly realistic Virtual Reality (VR) system powered by Large Language Models (LLMs), which enables rapid creation of dynamic Gaussian assets and real-time VR interactions. To inform our system design, we conducted interviews to examine challenges faced by current GS-based VR systems and the specific demands of users. Based on these insights, we employed GPT-4o to analyze key physical properties of objects that significantly impact user interactions, ensuring physics-based interactions in VR align with real-world phenomena. A key innovation of LIVE-GS is its ability to predict reasonable parameters in just 10 seconds from static Gaussian assets while maintaining high-quality VR interactions. To validate our approach, we invited participants experienced in physical simulation to manually adjust physical parameters, providing a baseline for comparison in both asset quality and authoring efficiency. We also conducted a comprehensive user study to evaluate system usability and user satisfaction. Experimental results demonstrate that LIVE-GS, leveraging LLMs' scene understanding capabilities, can achieve efficient physical scene creation and natural interactions without requiring manual design or annotation.

title: Fault Detection of Coil Misalignment in Multi-Receiver Wireless Power Transfer Systems With the Estimated Mutual Inductance
authors: Mingi Kim, Minseok Kang, Chang-Yeob Chu, Youngseok Lee, Sunyeol Gwon, In Gwun Jang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: In a multi-receiver wireless power transfer (WPT) system, mutual inductance is a key determinant of electrical performance. In small mobile-device applications, receivers (RXs) can be placed or moved arbitrarily on a single transmitter (TX) pad, making coil misalignment unavoidable; therefore, reliable fault detection is essential for stable operation. This study proposes a two-stage approach to estimate the full set of mutual inductances. In Stage 1, each receiver is sequentially activated with a fixed pulse width modulation duty cycle to estimate the TX–RX mutual inductance MTi and equivalent load resistance Req,i. In Stage 2, receiver pairs are activated under the same condition to estimate the RX–RX mutual inductance Mik. Then, misalignment faults can be detected by comparing the estimated mutual inductance values with predefined thresholds. A three-receiver prototype (10 mm airgap) validates accurate mutual inductance estimation and reliable fault detection.

title: 2025 IEEE Scientific Visualization Contest Winner: A Comprehensive Visual Exploration Tool for Multivariate Multi-Objective Optimization of Aluminum Alloy Compositions_supp1-3678413.mp4
authors: Reyhaneh Sabbagh Gol
publish: N/A
abstract: The exploration of scrap alloy compositions to discover new high-performance metals presents a significant challenge due to the large search space of chemical compositions and multi-objective material properties. We present an interactive visual analysis tool that allows for a comprehensive exploration of all facets of the data. We employ multivariate data visualization methods in coordinated views for overviews, detailed exploration, and comparison. A level-of-detail control allows for interactive sessions.

title: : A Plug-In Module Lifting MV Diffusion Models for 3D Asset Generation_supp2-3679384.mp4
authors: Jin Xie
publish: N/A
abstract: The labor- and experience-intensive creation of 3D assets with physically based rendering (PBR) materials demands an autonomous 3D asset creation pipeline. However, most existing 3D generation methods focus on geometry modeling, either baking textures into simple vertex colors or leaving texture synthesis to post-processing with image diffusion models. To achieve end-to-end PBR-ready 3D asset generation, we present Lightweight Gaussian Asset Adapter (LGAA), a novel framework that unifies the modeling of geometry and PBR materials by exploiting multi-view (MV) diffusion priors from a novel perspective. The LGAA features a modular design with three components. Specifically, the LGAA Wrapper reuses and adapts network layers from MV diffusion models, which encapsulate knowledge acquired from billions of images, enabling better convergence in a data-efficient manner. To incorporate multiple diffusion priors for geometry and PBR synthesis, the LGAA Switcher aligns multiple LGAA Wrapper layers encapsulating different knowledge. Then, a tamed variational autoencoder (VAE), termed LGAA Decoder, is designed to predict 2D Gaussian Splatting (2DGS) with PBR channels. Finally, we introduce a dedicated post-processing procedure to effectively extract high-quality, relightable mesh assets from the resulting 2DGS. Extensive quantitative and qualitative experiments demonstrate the superior performance of LGAA with both text- and image-conditioned MV diffusion models. Additionally, the modular design enables flexible incorporation of multiple diffusion priors, and the knowledge-preserving scheme effectively preseves the 2D priors learned on massive image dataset, which leads to data efficient finetuning to lift the MV diffuison models for 3D generation with merely 69k multi-view instances. Our code, pre-trained weights, and the dataset used will be publicly available via our project page: https://zx-yin.github.io/dreamlifting/.

title: A Dual Beam Electrically Compact Wrapped-around Ku-Band Series-Fed Array for Satphones
authors: R Akhil Karthik, Karthikeya G. S, Shiban K Koul, Ajay K. Poddar, Ulrich Rohde
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: This paper presents an electrically compact antenna element which operates in Ku-band targeting satphones. The proposed antenna is a series-fed array design, each series fed array is connected to an equal power divider network for gain enhancement which enables simultaneous operation of both the series fed array antennas, supporting dual beams. The element is wrapped around the corner space of a typical satphone. The antenna operates at 14.3 GHz and has an overall gain of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8. 8 ~ d B i}$</tex> for both the beams.

title: VisionDerm: Efficient Net-Optimized Deep Learning Model for Skin Disease and Vitamin Deficiency Detection Using Non-Dermoscopic Standard Images
authors: Sanidhya Khullar, Prakriti Prakriti, Parth Chaudhary, Vivek Agarwal
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: An innovative way to classify common skin diseases such as Psoriasis, Eczema and acne, using deep learning methods. Non-dermoscopic (digital camera) images have been used instead of dermatoscopic images which require specialised medical imaging technologies. The method proposed here increases the usability of the technique, enabling the application within the general population. The process for classifying the diseases is broken down into 4 stages: 1) Dataset Preparation 2) Image Preprocessing 3) Feature Extraction 4) Disease Classification. The first step uses Median Filtering to remove noise and other Non-Skin Image Artifacts from the Image, while also preserving the key characteristics of the skin. The Feature Extraction is completed using the Shearlet Transform (Multi-Scale Analysis) plus Blur-Trace (Energy)-wounds to identify surface irregularities and Directional Textures of the Skin. Disease Classification is performed by use of an EfficientNet (Convolutional Neural Network). The Model's performance is validated (evaluated) on a number of metrics; namely Accuracy, Precision, Recall and F1Score and cross-validated using the K-Fold method. The proposed approach will also assess Vitamin Deficiency by estimating the probability a person has a Vitamin Deficiency based on the skin texture features, however, due to the lack of a clinically validated Annotated Data Set, this part of the process cannot be quantitatively evaluated. In addition, as the proposed method is accessible to any one who owns a Digital Camera (or access to a Digital Camera).

title: Artificial Intelligence Based Virtual Assistant System
authors: J Shanthalakshmi Revathy, J Matcha Rani, M Ritika Sachdeva, T G Manasha Devi
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Students, parents, and guardians occasionally seek real and current information about college admissions, programs, tuition fees, examinations and facilities of college. The traditional means of information broadcasting by use of static web pages, frequently asked questions among other related systems might not always address the diverse and paraphrased questions of the learners. This paper uses natural language processing, identification of semantic intents, and rule-based reasoning to respond to conversations smarter. The suggested system can break down the queries posed by users in various linguistic forms and can respond human-like by semantic matching using keywords, addition of synonyms, and template response to queries. The proposed system can be implemented using a lightweight web-based application toolset that comprises of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript languages that do not need any backend resources and enjoy the benefit of easiness upon system deployment and maintenance. The experimental study of the system demonstrates an accuracy of 91 percentage and response time of less than one second thus demonstrating the efficiency and practicality of the system in offering real time response to the learner in the environment of higher learning institutions.

title: AI-Augmented Data Architecture: A Next-Generation Framework for Scalable, Intelligent, and Autonomous Data Ecosystems
authors: Kashif Mir Mazer Ali
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: The rapid growth of heterogeneous, high-velocity data and the increasing demand for autonomous, data-driven decision-making have exposed significant limitations in traditional data architectures, including static pipelines, limited adaptability, and high manual operational overhead. Existing models often treat artificial intelligence and machine learning as auxiliary components rather than integral elements of the data lifecycle, restricting scalability and autonomy. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an AI-Augmented Data Architecture (AADA), a unified and layered framework that embeds intelligence, automation, and governance across data ingestion, storage, processing, feature engineering, model orchestration, and feedback mechanisms. The proposed architecture leverages cloud-native design, microservices, container orchestration, and closed-loop learning to enable selfoptimization, scalability, and resilience in dynamic data environments. A reference implementation and empirical evaluation using high-velocity streaming workloads demonstrate improvements in scalability, model performance stability, latency control, and resource efficiency compared to conventional data pipelines. The study also discusses practical challenges related to governance, cost, ethical AI, and feedback risks, outlining future research directions for deploying fully autonomous and intelligent data ecosystems.

title: Actor-Critic Framework-Based on Optimal Tracking Strategy for Snake Robots with Reinforcement Learning Method
authors: Dongfang Li, Rong Hu, Yilong Zhang, Rob Law, Zhezhuang Xu, Suet To, Edmond Q. Wu, Limin Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Series Snake robots possess strong adaptability for unstructured environments, but their trajectory tracking control is hindered by nonlinear dynamics and model uncertainties. This article proposes an optimal tracking control strategy based on an actor–critic reinforcement learning framework. The method integrates line-of-sight guidance with serpentine gait generation, while a neural network identification system approximates the solution of the Hamilton–Jacobi–Bellman equation for unknown dynamics. Actor and critic networks are employed to update control policies and cost functions online, reducing dependence on precise models. Rigorous theoretical analysis proves that position and velocity errors achieve semi-global uniform ultimate boundedness. Both simulations and prototype experiments were conducted based on a servo-driven yaw-pitch linkage alternating series snake robot. The results verify that the proposed method can achieve accurate trajectory tracking, rapid convergence, and stable joint control, demonstrating its effectiveness and superiority compared to existing methods.

title: Synchronous Switching Scheme - Bringing Modular Fault Tolerance to Power Converters
authors: Aditya Shirodkar, Satish Naik Banavath, Andrii Chub, Riccardo Mandrioli, Mattia Ricco, Dimitri Vinnikov
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: Various critical applications in domains such as electric vehicles, space technology, defence, data-centers and microgrids require high power, uninterruptible and high reliability power electronic converters. This paper focusses on developing a highly modular and topology independent fault tolerant approach, making it an attractive solution for industrial applications. The paper validates the proposed approach through PLECS simulation of a 400 V and 5 kW full-bridge forward converter, and performs hardware validation at 400 V DC bus voltage as well. Further, the paper thoroughly analyses the losses, efficiency, reliability and mean time to failure of the proposed fault tolerance solution, and proves its effectiveness across various key factors in comparison to existing approaches in literature.

title: A Survey on Coded Matrix Computations and Gradient Coding
authors: Aditya Ramamoorthy, Anindya Bijoy Das
publish: IEEE BITS the Information Theory Magazine
abstract: In modern times, processing and storage needs have made distributed computing the technology of choice in various domains. How ever, large-scale distributed clusters (especially within the cloud) often suffer from the problem of stragglers (slow or failed workers). Coded distributed computation focuses on straggler mitigation ideas that have their roots in coding theory, i.e., it judiciously introduces redundancy across tasks assigned to workers such that the desired result can be obtained even in the presence of stragglers. Within distributed matrix computations and distributed parameter learning, it has led to key results on the trade-off between redundancy and the level of straggler mitigation that can be supported. In this survey, we overview the key ideas underpinning coded computation for both these applications. Following this, we offer insights about important considerations such as the numerical stability of recovery, leveraging partial work from slow (but not failed) workers, and considering scenarios where the inputs are sparse; we also discuss several works that have addressed these issues. We conclude with a discussion of open problems and future directions within these broad areas.
