title: Pinching-Antenna Systems (PASS) Meet Multiple Access: NOMA or OMA?
authors: Qiao Ren, Xidong Mu, Siyu Lin, Yuanwei Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: A fundamental two-user PASS-based communication system is considered under three MA schemes, namely non orthogonal multiple access (NOMA), frequency division multiple access (FDMA), and time division multiple access (TDMA). For each MA scheme, a pinching beamforming optimization problem is formulated to minimize the required transmit power for satisfying users' rate requirements. For NOMA and FDMA, a two-stage algorithm is proposed, where the locations of PAs are derived sequentially by using the successive convex approximation (SCA) method and fine-tuning phase adjustment. For TDMA, by leveraging the time-switching feature of PASS, the optimal pinching beamforming of each time slot is derived to maximize the served user channel gain. Numerical results are provided to show that: 1) PASS can achieve a significant performance gain over conventional antenna systems, and 2) NOMA consistently outperforms FDMA, while TDMA provides superior performance than NOMA for symmetric user rate requirements.

title: FNS-ID: A Federated Neuro-Symbolic Intrusion Detection System for Zero-Day Threat Detection in Mobile Applications
authors: Kammari Srujana, Nimmakayala Himani, Pabbisetty Nethra Vyshnavi, N Yoga Venkata Sai Kiran Achary
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Most users have entered the era of smartphone, thus they inevitably generate a lot of important short messages on the mobile platform and collecting them becomes an interesting topic. Current IDSs face the challenge of having partial threat signatures, as well as high rates of false alarms and room for exploring novel attack patterns. To mitigate the aforementioned challenges, to introduce FNS-ID, a Federated Neuro-Symbolic Intrusion Detection ID for on-the-fly discovery of zero-day threats in mobile settings. The system unifies federated learning for privacy-preserved distributed model training, a neurosymbolic reasoning engine that joints deep neural representations with an interpretable rule-based knowledge base, and a multi-modal behavioral analytic engine that analyzes network flows, system calls, application permissions and contextual device signals. Experimental results demonstrate that FNS-ID performs better than traditional machine learning and deep learning IDS models with accuracy of 96.7%, precision of 95.9%, recall of 96.1% and an F1-score of 96.0% as well as the zero-day attack detection rate is maintained at 92.2% for a small false positive ratio. A decentralized learning scheme allows better privacy preservation and generalization across diverse mobile devices. Our results show FNS-ID is a scalable, explainable and privacyaware security for next generation mobile application protection.

title: An Efficient Deep Learning Framework for Mental Health Indicator Detection from Social Media Text
authors: Ravikumar Gotthi, Ramaraju SVSV Palla, N.Kesava Rao, Modugula Sivajyothi, Siva Parvathi Jetti, A. Lakshmanarao
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Mental health concerns expressed on social media platforms have increased significantly, making automated analysis of textual content an important tool for early identification of mental health indicators. Social media text is often informal, noisy, and sequential in nature, posing challenges for traditional text classification approaches. This paper presents an efficient deep learning framework for detecting mental health indicators from social media text by formulating the task as a classification problem. The study evaluates machine learning models and deep learning architectures to analyze their effectiveness in capturing linguistic and contextual patterns associated with mental health-related expressions. Experimental results demonstrate that deep learning models outperform classical approaches, while hybrid architectures further enhance classification performance by jointly learning local textual patterns and longrange contextual dependencies. The findings highlight the suitability of hybrid deep learning models for robust mental health indicator detection from social media text.

title: Q-KVComm: Efficient Multi-Agent Communication via Adaptive KV Cache Compression
authors: Boris Kriuk, Logic Ng
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Multi-agent Large Language Model (LLM) systems face a critical bottleneck: redundant transmission of contextual information between agents consumes excessive bandwidth and computational resources. Traditional approaches discard internal semantic representations and transmit raw text, forcing receiving agents to recompute similar representations from scratch. We introduce Q-KVComm, a new protocol that enables direct transmission of compressed key-value (KV) cache representations between LLM agents. Q-KVComm combines three key innovations: (1) adaptive layer-wise quantization that allocates variable bit-widths based on sensitivity profiling, (2) hybrid information extraction that preserves critical facts across content domains, and (3) heterogeneous model calibration establishing cross-architecture communication. Extensive experiments across three diverse question-answering datasets demonstrate that QKVComm achieves 5-6x compression ratios while maintaining semantic fidelity, with coherence quality scores above 0.77 across all scenarios. The protocol exhibits robust performance across model sizes (1.1B-1.5B parameters) and adapts to real-world applications including conversational QA and multi-hop reasoning. Our work establishes a new paradigm for LLM agent communication, shifting from text-based to representation-based information exchange.

title: BPM-Net: A Robust Machine Learning Model for Bipolar Disorder Diagnosis
authors: Santosh Rani, Neeraj Mangla
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Bipolar Disorder (BD) is still considered one of the most difficult psychiatric disorders to diagnose because of symptom overlaps, mood swings, and lack of structured clinical evidence. Conventional Machine Learning (ML) methods provide only partial answers and often do not manage to deal with BD datasets' non-linear interactions, time dependencies, and very large class imbalances. The limitations are taken care of by introducing BPM-Net, a powerful ML framework that combines hierarchical feature extraction with an adaptive dualpath attention mechanism for precise BD detection. The model to enrich the diagnostic representations. For thorough benchmarking, BPM-Net is compared to not only a strong hybrid baseline model, Gaussian Mixture Models (GMM)SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique)XGBoost but also strong baseline hybrid ML models Decision Tree (DT), Logistic Regression (LR), Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM) and LSTM(Long Short Term Memory). The experiments conducted on a real-world BD dataset involved comprehensive preprocessing, normalization, and optimized hyperparameter tuning. BPM-Net surpassed the baseline models and achieved 98% accuracy. Moreover, attention heatmaps from BPM-Net foreground clinically significant features, which not only makes the system more interpretable but also increases clinician trust. This study shows that BPM-Net is both a clinically relevant and a computationally efficient diagnostic tool, which can lead to the early detection of BD, support personalized treatments, and be used for largescale AI-assisted psychiatric evaluation.

title: Robust, Real-Time Respiratory Rate Estimation using Wi-Fi Channel State Information
authors: S K Manikandan, M Nisha Angeline, V Suganya, Gokul Chandrasekaran
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Respiratory monitoring based on commodity WiFi equipment has been proposed as an alternative to wearable and camera-based systems since it preserves privacy, has low deployment costs, and can work well in low-light settings. In the present paper, a solid Channel State Information (CSI)based respiration monitoring model is proposed to work in the conditions of noise stress in an indoor environment. The system proposed multi-stage signal processing pipeline that includes adaptive subcarrier selection, spatial separation using principal component analysis (PCA), harmonic validation and using a confidence-weighted Kalman filter(CW-KF) to smooth the signal with time. Comprehensive synthetic-data-based tests are performed to examine the system robustness in the presence of different noise, multipath interference and distortion of signals. Besides this, a small-scale real-time demonstration based on an ESP32 Wi-Fi platform and a commercial patient monitor is also offered to make a qualitative comparison. Experimental findings demonstrate a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of less than 0.3 BPM in baseline conditions and 0.5 BPM under severe noise, with a sensing accuracy of 95% within a 1.5-meter range

title: Hybrid Deep Learning-Based Breast Cancer Detection Using VGG16 and ResNet50V2 Fusion Model
authors: Akash Raghuvanshi, Divyanshu Pathak, Ishita Verma
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Breast cancer is a huge health issue for women in the current world. Breast cancer must be found early if treatment is to work and lives are to be saved. I have watched doctors examine tissue samples under a microscope. The work takes time. Can lead to error. The project introduces a deep learning system. The deep learning system can automatically spot cancer in tissue images with precision. Our approach does not rely on a single AI model. Instead, we built a system that combines two architectures: VGG16 and ResNet50V2. The ensemble system works like a team of experts. The team of experts makes the final diagnosis more reliable. We added a meta-model to merge the information from both sides. The meta-model brings together the output from VGG16. The output from ResNet50V2. We also used image cleaning techniques (preprocessing) to remove blur and noise. Image cleaning techniques (preprocessing) let the AI see the details of the cells. We are pleased to see that the results of our study are good. The combined model performed better than any model used alone, and the combined model achieved high accuracy. The combined model is designed to be efficient, so the combined model does not need a supercomputer to run. The combined model can be used easily in hospitals. The framework offers a trustworthy tool for doctors. The framework helps doctors catch breast cancer early, and the framework improves the chances of getting better for patients.

title: HKT-SmartAudit: Distilling Lightweight Models for Smart Contract Auditing
authors: Zhiyuan Wei, Jing Sun, Zijian Zhang, Zhe Hou, Xianhao Zhang, Meng Li, Yuqiang Sun, Daoyuan Wu, Yang Liu, Chunmiao Li, Mingchao Wan, Jin Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: The rapid growth of blockchain technology has driven the widespread adoption of smart contracts; however, their inherent vulnerabilities have led to significant financial losses. Traditional auditing methods, while essential, struggle to keep pace with the increasing complexity and scale of smart contracts. Large language models (LLMs) offer promising capabilities for automating vulnerability detection, but their adoption is often limited by high computational costs. Although prior work has explored leveraging large models through agents or workflows, relatively little attention has been given to improving the performance of smaller, fine-tuned models—a critical factor for achieving both efficiency and data privacy. In this paper, we introduce HKT-SmartAudit, a framework for developing lightweight models optimized for smart contract auditing. It features a multi-stage knowledge distillation pipeline that integrates classical distillation, external domain knowledge, and reward-guided learning to transfer high-quality insights from large teacher models. A single-task learning strategy is employed to train compact student models that maintain high accuracy and robustness while significantly reducing computational overhead. Experimental results show that our distilled models outperform both commercial tools and larger models in detecting complex vulnerabilities and logical flaws, offering a practical, secure, and scalable solution for smart contract auditing. The source code is available in the GitHub repository<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>.

title: GNSS-Derived Sub-THz Water Vapor Attenuation Over a Tropical Climate for Earth-Space Links
authors: Gustavo A. Siles, Monserrat Urcullo
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: This study presents a seven-year time series of water vapor attenuation at 142 and 240 GHz in a tropical climate. Both frequencies are of interest for future NTN links. The methodology combines GNSS delay data from an IGS (International GNSS Service) station located in the Bolivian Amazon, ERA5 climate reanalysis data and the latest ITU-R model. Zenith attenuation peak values of up to 7.11 dB at 142 GHz and 21.36 dB at 240 GHz are observed primarily during summer, with high levels also noted in spring and fall. Slant-path links experiment even higher attenuation levels.

title: Design and Analysis of Circularly Polarized Antenna for Cubesats Operating at 868 MHz
authors: Vinicius Uchoa Oliveira, Pedro Pinho
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: CubeSats have democratized space applications, enabling diverse entities to pursue innovative missions, but their compact form imposes stringent design constraints, particularly for antennas. This paper presents a circularly polarized antenna design for CubeSats operating at 868 MHz. It is a high-gain patch antenna with a printed feed structure achieving 4.84 dBi gain and a 1.7 dB axial ratio. Simulated in CST Microwave Studio and adhering to CubeSat size constraints, these designs address trade-offs between gain and orientation independence, offering versatile solutions for reliable communication in small satellite missions.

title: Reconfigurable Antenna Arrays: Bridging Electromagnetics and Signal Processing
authors: Mengzhen Liu, Ming Li, Rang Liu, Qian Liu, A. Lee Swindlehurst
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: Reconfigurable antennas (RAs), capable of dynamically adapting their radiation patterns, polarization states, and operating frequencies, have emerged as a promising technology to meet the stringent performance requirements of sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks. This article systematically introduces essential hardware implementations of RAs and investigates advanced array architectures, such as fully-digital and tri-hybrid designs, emphasizing their capability to synergistically integrate electromagnetic (EM) reconfigurability with analog and digital signal processing. By facilitating coordinated beamforming across the EM and signal processing domains, RA arrays offer substantial flexibility and adaptability compared to conventional static antenna systems. Representative applications empowered by RA arrays, including integrated sensing and communication (ISAC), physical layer security (PLS), and near-field communications, are highlighted. A case study illustrates the effectiveness of RA arrays in optimizing beam steering, improving link robustness, and alleviating system power consumption. Finally, several open challenges and future research directions are outlined, emphasizing the need for advancements in theoretical modeling, hardware reliability, channel estimation techniques, intelligent optimization methods, and innovative network architectures, to fully realize the transformative impact of RAs in future 6G wireless networks.

title: Design of a Passive Load-Bearing Exoskeleton With Reduced Joint Misalignment and Leg Interaction Forces_supp4-3675446.mp4
authors: xinyu wu
publish: N/A
abstract: Load-bearing exoskeletons offer assistance in military, firefighting, and logistics fields. In such scenarios, weights are carried across uneven terrains. Therefore, an exoskeleton that efficiently transfers loads to the ground, adapts to versatile gaits, and minimally impacts leg movement is desired. This article presents a passive load-bearing exoskeleton to achieve these objectives. The passive exoskeleton operates without external power, transfers the load to the ground and redirects interaction forces from the thigh to the waist, and reduces potential impact on leg motion. A hip force-restoring mechanism and an optimized knee four-bar linkage were developed to minimize misalignment, enhancing human–machine kinematic compatibility. Experiments on five participants showed that the exoskeleton can reduce misalignment by 42.55% during load-bearing walking and 79.68% when squatting. The thigh interaction force was decreased by 55.65%, and load-transfer efficiency reached 60.10%. Compared to commercial load-bearing exoskeletons, there was a significant reduction in metabolic cost both indoors and outdoors, showing the exoskeleton's effectiveness in various terrain conditions.

title: Sub-GHz Compact Antenna Integrated with AMC for WBAN Applications
authors: Joyce Vanessa Morais Rodrigues, Alexandre Jean René Serres, Laila Fighera Marzall, Simone Genovesi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: This paper presents the design, fabrication, and evaluation of a compact, bio-inspired CPW antenna operating in the sub-GHz band, integrated with an Artificial Magnetic Conductor (AMC) and layers for WBAN applications, which stands out as a low-profile, low-cost solution. Miniaturization is achieved by combining a conductor based on Gielis'superformula and L-shaped inductors in the transmission line, resulting in resonance at 915 MHz. Experimental validation was performed using a multilayer phantom (comprising skin, fat, and muscle) with dielectric properties adjusted to match reference values. The simulation results indicated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left\vert \mathbf{S}_{\mathbf{1 1}}\right\vert=-\mathbf{2 1. 3 3 ~ d B}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 2 2 ~ M H z}$</tex>, while the measurements showed a shift to 946.6 MHz with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left\vert \mathrm{S}_{11}\right\vert=-17.42 \text{dB}$</tex> for the antenna in free space. The results confirm the antenna's potential for low-frequency biomedical applications, with high efficiency and adequate operation close to the human body.

title: Cotton Disease Detection Using MutilAO Segmentation and Deep Learning Classification
authors: E. D. Pavanakumar, S. Malathi, C. Siva Balaji Yadav
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: According to this study, deep learning models may be able to see hurt leaves on cotton plants by looking at pictures of them as they grow. We need to take care of these things well. Background: Deep Learning can be used to find and spot plant diseases early on from pictures of leaves. There is a link between this area of study and farming. The Indian government says agriculture makes up 17% of the country's GDP. Objective: The main goal of this study is to help solve the problem and show how deep Learning can be used to screen cotton leaves. Now, we can keep an eye on the health of the cotton growth and decide how best to take care of it. Methods and results: To find out what plants are sick with is a very important use of deep Learning. It is based on metaDeep Learning. For this study, we had to take pictures of cotton leaves in the field. The set has <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 3, 3 8 0}$</tex> pictures of leaves that are both healthy and sick. This method made the collection bigger so that the new material could fit. We used Custom CNN, VGG16 Transfer Learning, ResNet50, and the model we showed to train the dataset: the meta-deep learn leaf disease detection model. People have devised a metalearning method that is being used to get very good accuracy and spread. The program got the accuracy 98.53% of the time, which was better than the Cotton Dataset.

title: Multi-Scale Deep Transfer Learning and Attention-Guided Feature Fusion for Robust Cervical Cell Categorization
authors: J Pradeep, M Harikrishnan, Sanjay P, Mohamed Siddiq R, Shivsharan N
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Conventional cervical cancer diagnosis, including Pap smear and histopathological examination, is frequently subjective, time-consuming and variable. To tackle the above limitations, in this paper, this work presents a robust AI-based framework for automated cervical cell classification, termed Multi-Scale Deep Transfer Learning and Attention-Guided Feature Fusion. The model combines several pre-trained CNN models (EfficientNetB0, InceptionV3, ConvNeXt-Tiny, Swin Transformer, DenseNet121, and MobileNetV3-Large) to generate multi-scale features. An attention guided feature fusion module with SE and CBAM mechanisms is utilized to improve the feature representation by focusing on the diagnostic-related regions. The addition of Focal Loss with Label Smoothing enhances class balance management and mitigates overconfidence. In addition, the stacked RBM network in the proposed hybrid deep learning method can be visually explained by Grad-CAM heatmaps, providing incremental value not only in boosting the classification accuracy and its generalization for imbalanced datasets but also visual explanatory capability. The proposed framework achieves a classification accuracy of 97.4%, with precision of 97.1%, recall of 97.6%, and an F 1 score of 97.3% on the SIPaKMeD dataset. Thus, the proposed scheme offers a scalable, data-driven, and noninvasive approach to the accurate early screening of cervical cancer.

title: V-Plaque Network: Hybrid AI Framework for High Precision Detection of Vulnerable Atherosclerosis
authors: A. Inbavalli, S. Asvini, C. Manimegalai, D. Jayakumar, S. Nithyabharathi, A. Balachandar
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases, which are the leading cause of death in the world, are caused in most cases by atherosclerosis, whose current diagnosis using ultrasound is still operator-dependent and does not allow for a reliable discrimination between stable plaques and high-risk plaques that are vulnerable to rupture. To overcome this severe limitation, we propose a new and AI-based diagnostic pipeline based on a hybrid multi-stage pipeline of TransUNet for segmentation, a multi-input CNN with attention for fusion of Bmode, Doppler and Elastography for plaque characterization, and ensemble XGBoost classifier with derived biomechanical parameters, such as WSS for final interpretive diagnosis. The cross verified approach leads to a new level of accuracy: 98% detection of vulnerable plaques, revolutionizing the standard of care, taking the subjective interpretation and changing it to an objective, automated and highly accurate screening tool. Our solution also offers fast non-invasive screening for vulnerabilities, giving clinicians the power to intervene earlier, giving personalized treatment plans, and scaling on existing ultrasound systems to prevent deaths and disabilities worldwide.

title: Hybrid Model Approach for Chronic Kidney Disease Classification
authors: Chelsea Leawana, Richard Manuel Sunanto, Said Achmad, Derwin Suhartono
publish: 2025 IEEE International Biomedical Instrumentation and Technology Conference (IBITeC)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is a public health issue often undetected in its early stages and followed by delayed treatment. An accurate and accessible classifier is essential to reduce the risk of delayed healthcare intervention. This study proposes a hybrid machine learning model approach for detecting CKD by integrating traditional and ensemble learning methods. The hybrid model utilizes five base classifiers such as XGBoost, AdaBoost, Random Forest, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Support Vector Machine (SVM) followed by a hard voting strategy to determine the majority class. Furthermore, a stacking ensemble method was implemented using Logistic Regression as meta-learner to elevate the performance. Experimental results showed that Random Forest, XGBoost, and AdaBoost achieved the highest accuracy of 0.991, recall of 0.986, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of 0.993, which were similar to the Voting and Stacking models. KNN and SVM produced lower scores, indicating difficulty in learning complex feature interactions. These findings suggest that simpler ensemble models can perform as effectively as hybrid approaches while requiring less computational cost and being easier to interpret. However, due to the small dataset size, there is a risk of overfitting. Future work should focus on larger datasets, external validation, and explainable AI methods to improve reliability in clinical applications.

title: AVATAR-MUSE: An Automated, Avatar-Based Chatbot System for Museum Ticket Booking
authors: Pranay Jha, S. Pradeep Kumar, S. Manoj, A. Sinduja, Suganiya Murugan
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: The booking of museum tickets is a critical yet often cumbersome process for cultural enthusiasts, tourists, and casual visitors alike. Conventional web portals for museum reservations are frequently text-heavy, cluttered, and unintuitive, leading to poor user experience and decreased accessibility. This paper presents AVATAR-MUSE, a next-generation, automated, webbased system that revolutionizes the ticket booking process by combining an intelligent conversational AI with a visually engaging, avatar-driven interface. Leveraging advanced Natural Language Processing (NLP) and deep learning models, AVATARMUSE interprets diverse user inputs, understands conversational context, and responds with relevant booking information in real-time. Unlike static booking websites, AVATAR-MUSE incorporates a human-like avatar, powered by WebGL-based rendering and motion animation, to guide users through the process in a friendly and intuitive manner. The system is tightly integrated with museum APIs to fetch live data about exhibitions, available time slots, ticket prices, and booking statuses. Additionally, it supports secure, real-time payment processing and booking confirmation, ensuring a seamless end-toend user journey. By merging voice/text input processing, secure backend architecture, and visually immersive interaction, AVATAR-MUSE redefines how users engage with cultural institutions online. This paper details the architecture, implementation, and evaluation of the system and highlights its potential to be extended across other domains such as theaters, concerts, and heritage tourism.

title: Vegetation Height Estimation Using a 77-80 GHz Millimeter-Wave Radar
authors: Daniel Espinal-de-la-Cruz, Mark Clemente-Arenas, Jinmi Lezama-Calvo, Sleiter Ramos-Sanchez
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: This paper investigates a low-cost <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$77-80 \text{GHz}$</tex> FMCW millimeter-wave radar workflow for short-range forest canopy-height estimation. Radar echoes collected along linear trajectories in front of real trees are processed with a standard FMCW chain (range FFT, log-magnitude, smoothing, and CFAR) to generate a point cloud and a simple ground profile. Unsupervised clustering (DBSCAN, OPTICS, and Mean Shift) is then used to isolate canopy returns and estimate per-tree height as the difference between the canopy envelope and the ground surface. Experiments on three trees <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(3.36 ~\mathrm{m}-3.45 ~\mathrm{m})$</tex> show that DBSCAN and OPTICS achieve errors of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.2 ~\mathrm{m}-0.3 ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> for two trees, while Mean Shift tends to merge nearby crowns and yields a larger error (0.6 m). These results suggest that FMCW mmWave radar combined with clustering can provide canopy-height estimates within a few decimeters for local forest monitoring when airborne LiDAR is impractical.

title: Fast Human-Robot Handover using a Vision-Based Pipeline_supp1-3680049.pdf

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning based Deferred Entanglement Path Selection in Quantum Networks
authors: Xiaoyu Wang, Yangming Zhao, Enshu Wang, Chen Tian, Kun Yang, Chunming Qiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: Conventional entanglement routing approaches decide the Entanglement Paths (EPs) to establish Entanglement Connections (ECs) before trying to create Entanglement Links (ELs). By doing so, very few EL failures will result in a low network throughput. In this paper, we study how to choose the EPs to establish ECs after knowing which ELs are successfully created. This is called the Deferred EP Selection (DEPS) problem. DEPS is a generalized integer multi-commodity flow problem and we cannot solve it quickly with conventional optimization methods. To address this issue, we propose a Deep Reinforcement Learning based EP Selection (DRLEPS) approach. The salient features of DRLEPS include (i) by controlling the number of candidate EPs, DRLEPS can achieve a trade-off between time complexity and the EC establishment rate; and (ii) using candidate EPs as input, DRLEPS is robust to request variation; and (iii) by training neural networks with different topologies, a model derived by DRLEPS can be applied to various networks (even with a different number of nodes) without fine-tune. Through extensive simulations, we show that even in a network with 200 nodes, DRLEPS can solve the DEPS problem in 0.39 seconds with a Nvidia GeForce 3090 GPU. It outperforms the approach always establishing ECs through the EP with the largest success probability by up to 23.4% in EC establishment rate. It also outperforms the Integer Linear Programming (ILP) based scheme, which can achieve the maximum EC establishment rate, by up to 184.2x in network throughput.

title: WattWhisper: An AI-Enabled IoT Platform for Real-Time Electrical Load Identification and Overload Protection
authors: A. Arul, Sachin Deepak. S, S. Sabarinath, Rohith P
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Intelligent energy management and device control have become possible due to the quick development of smart home technology. In this paper, WattWhisper, an AI-powered smart outlet that uses Internet of Things technology to track power consumption, detect linked devices, and stop power outages instantly. For remote monitoring and control, the system consists of an INA219 current and voltage sensor, an ESP8266 microcontroller, and a relay module connected to the Blynk cloud platform. By analyzing the unique electrical patterns-or current signatures-of connected devices, a cloud-based AI model can recognize appliances such as lights or fans and detect irregular consumption that might signal an overload or inefficiency. When such conditions occur, WattWhisper automatically switches off the power through its relay and immediately alerts the user via the Blynk app. Experimental tests confirm that WattWhisper can reliably distinguish between common household appliances and react instantly to unsafe situations. By merging IoT connectivity with machine learning, the system promotes smoother interaction between humans and devices, enhancing safety, energy efficiency, and convenience. Overall, this work demonstrates how AI-powered IoT solutions can drive smarter, safer, and more sustainable energy management in modern homes.

title: AI as a Catalyst for Improved Outcomes in Infrastructure Development: Measurement of Key Variables Using Multi-Model
authors: Samarth Sharma, Ved Prakash, Meghna Sharma, Jimnee Dake, Aastha Anand
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: This research paper puts an effort to analyses meaningful relationship impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on Infrastructure development outcomes by using Structural Equation Model approach. The research followed the descriptive and cross-sectional approach, collecting data from 750 construction professionals. Five AI-enabled dimensions of infrastructure performance were tested in this study using PLS-SEM. There is a significant and positive impact of all the AI dimensions on IDC as observed from the results. AI-based Project Planning and Design was the leading contributor. The results of the study support AI as an enabling technology for better efficiency and decision-making in infrastructure projects.

title: A Robust Active Noise Control System Based on Logarithmic Hyperbolic Secant Adaptive Filter
authors: Gunuru Harsha Vardhan Naidu, Krishna Kumar
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Conventional active noise control (ANC) methods experience performance deterioration when exposed to impulsive noise (IN). Despite its transient nature, IN poses significant risks to auditory health. Recent approaches employing the maximum correntropy criterion (MCC) and its generalized version (GMCC) have attracted considerable interest for impulsive noise suppression. Nevertheless, these methods exhibit substantial steady-state misalignment, degrading their noise cancellation performance. To enhance ANC performance under impulsive conditions, this work introduces a novel robust filtered-x logarithmic hyperbolic secant adaptive filter (FxLHSAF) that minimizes a logarithmic hyperbolic secant loss function. Furthermore, an affine combination of FxLHSAF (A-FxLHSAF) is developed to attain accelerated convergence and reduced residual error. Experimental simulations demonstrate the superiority of both proposed techniques over other existing algorithms, with FxLHSAF and A-FxLHSAF delivering optimal noise suppression performance.

title: One Size Fits All: Achieving Generic and Communication-Efficient Federated Multi-Task Learning via Bi-level Sparsification
authors: Wentai Wu, Chiyuan Tan, Zhetao Li, Saiqin Long, Pengpeng Qiao, Zhirun Zheng, Weiwei Lin, Yang Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Federated Multi-Task Learning (FMTL) has demon-strated significant potential in benefiting data owners that per-form different tasks collaboratively in privacy-sensitive scenarios. However, existing FMTL methods struggle with the negative transfer that arises from the knowledge interference especially with over-parameterized models. This hinders both efficient knowledge transfer and the model's ability to capture inter-task correlations. To address these issues, we cast light on the power of sparsity, atop the highly modular Multi-gate Mixture-of-Experts (MMoE), to simultaneously realize effective separation and communication-efficient exchange of cross-domain knowl-edge over a generic architecture. With bi-level sparsification mechanism combining sparse expert activation with gradient-driven parameter masking, our approach offers a generic, extend-able, and interpretable framework by simultaneously achieving (1) structural mitigation of task interference by isolating knowl-edge pathways, and (2) significant reduction of communication overhead without architectural adaptation or explicit compres-sion. Experimental results demonstrate that, without relying on heavy models, our framework improves both model accuracy and convergence speed while cutting communication traffic by approximately 70% via the bi-level sparsification design.

title: Optimal Closed-Loop Control of Dual-Active Bridge Type DC-DC and Single-Stage DC-AC Converters With Multiple Degrees-of-Freedom
authors: Ivan Z. Petrić, Rahul Mallik, Milan Ilić
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: This paper introduces a method for optimal closed-loop control of DAB-type DC-DC and single-stage high-frequency link DC-AC converters with multiple degrees-of-freedom (DoF). The proposed single-input multiple-output (SIMO) architecture distributes the control effort among the DoF based on the gradient of the regulated output. This minimally perturbs the control input vector, thus reducing the jump discontinuities in the control variables and suppressing transient oscillations. Additionally, the SIMO control normalizes the loop gain to impose constant dynamics throughout the operating range. Finally, a saturation-handling algorithm is proposed to retain optimal distribution even if a control variable is saturated, allowing full controllability and constant small-signal dynamics as long as at least one DoF remains. A small-signal model is derived and validated in simulations. Experimental validations are presented on an 850 W series-resonant DAB prototype that is re-configurable for DC-DC and DC-AC operation.

title: Feeling the Facts: Real-time wearable fact-checkers can use nudges to reduce user belief in false information
authors: Chitralekha Gupta, Nadia Victoria Aritonang, Dixon Prem Daniel Rajendran, Valdemar Danry, Pattie Maes, Suranga Nanayakkara
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems

title: A Novel Framework for Analyzing CV–CC Nonlinear Charge–Discharge Dynamics in Battery Health Prediction
authors: Peiwen Zhong, Hongchi Zhou, Jianle Zheng, Yang Song, Yu Shi
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: Accurate SOH prediction is vital for lithium-ion battery management, but conventional data-driven methods using voltage, current, and temperature often overlook internal degradation. To addres this, the paper proposes a feature based on the ratio of CV charging time to CC discharging time, which captures battery degradation dynamics and reveals insights into resistance growth and capacity fade. The feature is incorporated into both multi-output Gaussian Process (MOGP) and long shortterm memory (LSTM) models to predict battery health, significantly improving prediction accuracy and robustness. Results show that, in MOGP, RMSE is reduced by 19.4% for LQd and 17.4% for cycle life; in LSTM, by 11.0% for LQd and 10.9% for cycle life. The improvement is more notable in MOGP, while LSTM maintains lower absolute RMSE and stronger robustness. Future work will explore physical meaning and broader applicability.

title: Fundamental Experiments to Enhance the Performance of Airport Runway Foreign Object Debris Detection Systems Through Multiplexed 96 GHz Millimeter-Wave Radar Coverage
authors: Shunichi Futatsumori, Kazuyuki Morioka, Akiko Kohmura, Noriaki Hiraga, Masahiko Sato, Naruto Yonemoto
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: Foreign object debris (FOD) detection systems at airports enhance runway safety and operational efficiency. We developed an FOD detection system based on a 96 GHz millimeter-wave radar. This paper proposes a method to improve the performance of the FOD detection system by multiplexing the coverage areas of the millimeter-wave radar. This approach reduces the number of required radar sensors and increases the FOD detection margin. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, fundamental experiments are conducted using a prototype system at Tokyo International Airport. The results confirm that by multiplexing the coverage areas of two radars, the coverage can be extended from 350 to more than 450 m.

title: The Research on the Prediction Method of Thermocouple Remaining Lifetime based on Reliability Analysis
authors: Yuchen Xie, Kang Xu, Yanhui Wang, Xianghui Chen, Linjiang Feng, Yan Ran
publish: 2025 8th World Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Intelligent Manufacturing (WCMEIM)
abstract: With the increasing demand for high-temperature precision measurements in the industrial and nuclear energy sectors, thermocouples, as critical temperature sensors, are widely used in various extreme environments. However, during prolonged operation, the key components of thermocouples (such as welding joints) are at risk of gradual failure due to factors such as material degradation and thermal fatigue. Therefore, accurately predicting the remaining lifetime of thermocouples is of significant importance for enhancing the reliability and safety of systems. This paper integrates reliability analysis methods for thermocouples, employing Failure Modes, Effects, and Criticality Analysis and Fault Tree Analysis to identify failure modes, and utilizes reliability models to evaluate their remaining lifetime. The study demonstrates that by establishing a systematic lifetime prediction framework and incorporating multiple failure mechanism models, the remaining lifetime of thermocouples can be effectively assessed under complex conditions such as high temperatures and irradiation, providing a scientific basis for thermocouple design optimization, maintenance planning, and fault prognostics.

title: Non-Destructive PCB Sub-mm Inspection Using Atomic Magnetometry
authors: Junghoon Oh, Hyun Joon Lee, Kyeseok Yoon, In-Kui Cho
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: This paper presents a radio-frequency atomic magnetometer (RFAM) system for high-resolution, contactless inspection of printed circuit boards (PCBs) with sub-millimeter trace spacing in the range of 0.2-0.8mm. Secondary magnetic fields at the femtotesla (fT) level, generated by eddy currents induced in conductive PCB traces by an externally applied primary magnetic field, were precisely detected using the RFAM. A rubidium vapor - based RFAM was employed, and a sensitivity of approximately 250 fT/$\sqrt{ }$Hz was achieved. Optical pumping was performed using diode lasers tuned to 780 nm (probe) and 795 nm (pump), while variations in the Larmor precession frequency of atomic spins were measured through polarization modulation techniques. Experimental validation was carried out, and the proposed system was shown to resolve PCB trace separations as small as 0.2 mm and to identify discontinuities such as breaks in a fully contactless manner. The measured magnetic field distributions were observed to correspond closely with the actual PCB layouts, confirming the reliability of the sensing system. Background noise subtraction was demonstrated to further improve spatial resolution and to reveal fine-scale, spatially independent magnetic field features. In addition, contour map analysis enabled quantitative estimation of inter-trace distances, and five distinct closed loops were identified, confirming the absence of electrical continuity between specific patterns. These results indicate that RFAMbased sensing can serve as a promising alternative to conventional non-destructive evaluation (NDE) techniques, offering distinct advantages including ultra-high sensitivity, contactless operation, and applicability to embedded or shielded structures.

title: High Quality Factor Microwave Sensor Using Interdigital Capacitor for Permittivity Measurement
authors: Isau de Sousa Silva, Ruann V. A. Lira, Isaac B. T. Da Silva, Antonio Luiz P. S. Campos
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd Latin American Conference on Antennas and Propagation (LACAP)
abstract: This work presents the design, simulation, and experimental validation of a high-quality factor microwave sensor for measuring the relative permittivity of solid materials. The proposed sensor integrates an interdigital capacitor (IDC) into a rectangular microstrip patch antenna. The sensor achieved a quality factor of 440, a frequency detection resolution (FDR) of 62 MHz, and an average measurement error of only 0.07%. Numerical simulations and equivalent circuit modeling confirm the accuracy of the sensor's behavior, while experimental measurements using five materials under test (MUTs) validate its practical performance.

title: AI-Powered Real-Time Workout Pose Analysis and Correction System
authors: Juhi Singh, Ansh Parnami, Nakul Dagar, Mohit Singh Adhikari
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The current 2020s global shift to fitness at home, which has been accelerated by the COVID-19 pandemic, has shown one very obvious need in the digital wellness industry: the delivery of corrective feedback in real-time and on the individual level. It is this demonstration-correction gap, which makes the report, which results in musculoskeletal injury, a significant risk to those who engage in exercise and associated injury prevention. In fact, studies have shown that misplaced exercise form is the leading cause of injuries. To overcome this barrier, this paper introduces Artificial Intelligence-based pose recognition and correction system, which is expected to serve as a virtual trainer to perform basic static exercises. Only a typical webcam is needed to make the system run, which is why it is widely accessible. The computationally efficient pipeline based on which this methodology is focused involves the use of Google MediaPipe skeletal landmark detection framework to detect 33 skeletal points with high fidelity. The domain-specific feature engineering converts raw landmark data into a standardized and pose-invariant feature image. Final classification is done by a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier which was trained on an annotated dataset of their own. Intensive rigor in quantitative assessment of the system shows it to have a high level of efficacy, with high F1-score of 94 and with a settled 30 Frames Per Second (FPS) and around 28 milliseconds latency on consumer-grade hardware.

title: Vehicle-to-Vehicle Millimeter-Wave Channel Characterization at 60 and 80 GHz
authors: Radek Zavorka, Jiri Blumenstein, Tomas Mikulasek, Josef Vychodil, Hussein Hammoud, Wojtuń Jarosław, Aniruddha Chandra, Markus Hofer, Jan M. Kelner, Cezary Ziółkowski, Christoph Mecklenbräuker, Ales Prokes
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: This paper presents results from a vehicle-to-vehicle channel measurement campaign conducted in the millimeter-wave (MMW) frequency bands at center frequencies of 60 GHz and 80 GHz, each with a bandwidth of 2 GHz. The measurements were performed in a dynamic oncoming-vehicle scenario using a time-domain channel sounder with high-resolution data acquisition. Power delay profiles were extracted to study the temporal evolution of multipath components, and the root mean square (RMS) delay spread was analyzed to characterize the temporal dispersion of the channel. The results demonstrate differences between the two frequency bands. At 60 GHz, the RMS delay spread is well approximated by a Gaussian distribution with a higher median value, while at 80 GHz it follows a lognormal distribution with a lower median. Furthermore, the number of resolvable multipath components was found to be nearly twice as high at 60GHz compared to 80 GHz, highlighting the impact of antenna beamwidth and frequency-dependent propagation mechanisms.

title: Broadbeam Crossed-Dipole Antenna Using Angularly Symmetric High Impedance Surface
authors: Bo Huang, Yu-Xiang Sun, Zengtai Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: This paper proposes a broadbeam crossed-dipole antenna using an angular symmetric high impedance surface (ASHIS). The crossed-dipole antenna is fed by a coaxial line and a pair of quarter-wavelength curved lines to generate circularly polarized (CP) radiation. Unlike conventional designs with simple metal ground, the proposed antenna employs an ASHIS to enhance the half-power beamwidth (HPBW). By tuning the reflection phase of each concentric split ring, the ASHIS can be equivalently projected as a concave surface, resulting in broadbeam radiation patterns. Simulation results show that the antenna achieves a −10-dB impedance bandwidth of 92.1% (0.79–2.24 GHz) and a 3-dB axial ratio bandwidth of 39.4% (1.30–1.92 GHz). Moreover, an HPBW greater than 136° is obtained at 1.575 GHz.

title: Analysis of the Influence of High-Proportion Distributed Generation Integration on Upstream and Downstream Location of Voltage Sag Sources
authors: Peng Wang, Fuchun Li, Yuanhui Sun, Jianxin Bi, Chunfeng Liu, Wenhuan Cheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: With the integration of high-proportion power electronics equipment and high-proportion renewable energy into the distribution system, the dynamic characteristics have undergone significant changes. The theoretical models of traditional upstream and downstream location methods for voltage sag sources are not targeted and lack sufficient accuracy. Addressing the aforementioned issues, this paper focuses on the impact of voltage sag sources in distribution networks containing distributed energy resources on traditional upstream and downstream location methods.

title: Low-Cost Antenna Gain Enhancement Using Inkjet-Printed Partially Reflective Surfaces
authors: Francesco Anelli, Antonella Maria Loconsole, Md. Imran Khan, Federico Moscatelli, Ahsan U. Khan, Federica Anfuso, Olga Basile, Francesco Prudenzano
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: This contribution illustrates the design, fabrication, and experimental validation of an inkjet-printed partially reflective surface integrated with a low-profile antenna to achieve gain enhancement. The proposed partially reflective surface is fabricated via cost-effective inkjet printing process, offering advantages in terms of rapid prototyping and low material wastage. To obtain a high reflection coefficient, additional polyethylene terephthalate glycol (PETG) layers have been introduced. The feeding antenna-partially reflective surface configuration operates as a Fabry-Pérot resonator antenna, where the spacing between the partially reflective surface and the ground plane is carefully optimized to support resonance and reinforce directive radiation. Simulation and measurement results demonstrate a gain improvement of about 5 dBi over the feeding antenna without the partially reflective surface, with minimal impact on impedance bandwidth. This work validates the viability of inkjet printing and additive manufacturing techniques for developing high-performance, lightweight, and low-cost antenna systems.

title: Phaseless EM field reconstruction: from deterministic phase retrieval to sparsity-based antenna diagnostics
authors: Tommaso Isernia, Andrea Francesco Morabito, Roberta Palmeri, Giada Maria Battaglia
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: This paper briefly reviews two research lines on phaseless electromagnetic field reconstruction investigated by our research group, and the corresponding outcomes. In particular, the first one deals with the very tough problem of retrieving complex fields from single-plane magnitude-only data. The second, leveraging sparsity assumptions and tools, is instead devoted to antenna diagnostics, with particular emphasis on fault detection in arrays. Three different approaches (with several options for the last and maybe final solution) are reviewed for the first problem, while the theoretical basis and the (simple) procedures are recalled as far as the second problem is concerned. Some possible future developments are also briefly envisaged.

title: Characterization of Antenna Array Properties Using Radar Cross Section (RCS) Technique from 2 to 18 GHz
authors: Habiba Hafdallah Ouslimani, Patricai Grassin Leze
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: This paper presents a non-invasive method for characterizing antenna elements based on radar cross-section (RCS) data. Measurements and simulations of the backscattered parameters, conducted in both monostatic and bistatic configurations, are performed by modulating the antenna’s electric boundary conditions. Accurate calibration enables elimination of the antenna’s structural mode, facilitating the extraction of the reradiated antenna mode and retrieval of key parameters, including gain, input impedance, reflection coefficient, and radiation patterns. Unlike conventional vector network analyzer (VNA) measurements, this method enables contactless characterization, making it particularly advantageous for small antennas or scenarios where direct access to the antenna is limited or unavailable.

title: Evaluating the Robustness of Machine Learning Models Against Adversarial Cyber Attacks
authors: Amit Kumar, Akash Rajak, Shashank Bhardwaj, Shruti Aggarwal, Naina Sharma, Annu Yadav
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: ML algorithms are having great use in detecting unseen or malicious network traffic as a default mechanism for implementing IDS or Intrusion Detection System. On the other hand, artistically crafted deviations in the traffic may lead to substantial degradation in the performance of IDS, and hence are supposed to be addressed seriously.

title: Institutional Readiness and Transformational Barriers: Artificial Intelligence Adoption Frameworks for Organizational Delivery Capability
authors: Vipul Razdan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: The study evidently shows the corporate sector has adopted the artificial intelligence technologies at a significantly slower pace in comparison to the adoption of similar technologies in other sectors like healthcare, retail, and manufacturing operations. Therefore, critical questions are raised regarding the organizational culture, technological and structural barriers that stand between the corporate sector and the transformation of the organisation project delivery through AI. This paper looks at why AI capabilities and the project management community do not seem to be systematically using intelligent automation in planning, executing, monitoring and controlling projects. By analyzing the strategies current applied by the AI vendors (foundational models vs. autnomous bespoke systems), the research identifies decision factors critical to an organisation's eligibility for AI within a project context: capital investment requirements, data architecture maturity, data set quality standards, scalability potential across project types, and risk tolerance thresholds. The paper categorizes inherent implementation challenges into technical, financial, and organizational domains. For instance, concern with model adequacy across heterogeneous project types. Cost-prohibitiveness for small-to-medium enterprises. Risk of ethical governance of autonomous decision-making in project contexts. For instance, marginalization of resource-constrained organizations lacking sufficient data infrastructure. Besides, the research looks into transitional pathways that designate domainspecific AI foundation models as intermediary stepping stones for gradual adaptation of organizations and capability development, as well as the future role and skill set evolution of project managers in algorithmic decision environments. The successful implementation of AI in project management necessitates an accompanying process of investment in the technology, a maturity assessment of one's organization, development of ethical frameworks, and adaptation strategies for the workforce. It is not simply a technical implementation.

title: Research on Fault Monitoring Method for Power Wireless Private Network Communication Terminals Based on VMD-OIF-BP
authors: Shuhao Lang, Di Liu, Yanru Wang, Zhigang Wang, Zhentao Shen
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: To effectively detect faults in power communication terminals, we introduce the VMD-OIF-BP algorithm designed to identify typical issues such as cable insulation damage, power supply interference, loose connectors, and chip burnout. The method begins by applying Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) to break down sensor signals and extract high-frequency intrinsic mode function (IMF) components. Next, the Outlier Identification Filter (OIF) removes anomalies from the data, which is then input into a backpropagation (BP) neural network for classification. Experimental findings show that this model attains an accuracy of 95.2% and a recall rate of 94.9%, surpassing the performance of BP, OIF, and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) models. Overall, the proposed technique demonstrates high fault detection accuracy and strong practical effectiveness.

title: Enhancing Hydro-Wind-PV Complementary Systems through Optimal Battery Storage Sizing
authors: Zhiyuan Leng, Lu Chen, Fanqian Liu, Bin Yi, Lei Yin, Cheng Ge
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: The variability of wind and photovoltaic generation poses significant challenges to system balance, even with hydropower complementarity. Battery energy storage systems provide a flexible solution, but their sizing must be carefully optimized. This study proposes a day-ahead generation and reserve scheduling model for hydro-wind-PV complementary systems with battery storage, aiming to maximize net complementary profits under renewable generation uncertainty. A chance-constrained optimization framework is applied to evaluate battery storage sizing configurations at the Lianghekou hydro-wind-PV base. The results demonstrate that battery storage integration significantly enhances economic performance, with optimal operation achieved at 380 MW/380 MWh. Moderate oversizing of energy capacity (e.g., 270 MW/540 MWh) remains effective, whereas excessive scaling leads to diminishing returns. This work provides practical guidance for the cost-effective integration of battery storage in large-scale renewable energy systems.

title: An Interpretable Machine Learning Framework for Anxiety Detection
authors: Devanshi Gaur, Loveleen Gaur
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: We investigated and assessed the SWELL Knowledge Work Dataset's HRV characteristics for anxiety identification. The model used is linear classification model under five-fold stratified cross-validation. The subsequent performance is average but constant, that reflects the intrinsic similarity between Anxiety and non-Anxiety autonomic responses rather than model overfitting. The feature importance and distributional analyses are also conducted to highlight the influence of cardiac timing and interim variability measures that are constant with proven autonomic physiology. The results demonstrated that transparent models could catch significant Anxiety-related signals from HRV signals and thus create a careful and physiology-conscious methodology to Anxiety analytics.

title: Research on Power Load Forecasting Based on VMD-CNN-GRU-ATTENTION Model
authors: Jianwei Cao, Xi Luo, Luosong Jin, Xiao Wei, Xiaoyu Yu, Zeying Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: The current power load is being influenced by multiple factors, showing strong volatility, non-stationarity and periodicity, which poses a significant challenge to the safety, stability and economic performance of the power system. For the accuracy and stability issues of load forecasting, this paper proposes a denoising CNN-GRU-ATTENTION power load forecasting method based on VMD. Firstly, the VMD is utilized to remove the noise in the load data and effectively improve the quality of the input data. Secondly, by constructing the CNN-GRU network prediction model and using the CNN model to enhance the feature extraction ability, accurate prediction of power load can be achieved. Meanwhile, the attention mechanism is introduced to enhance the model's ability to process key information. Lastly, the dataset of a particular Australian electrical market was used to confirm the model's validity. The results demonstrate that the suggested model has significant generalization capabilities and excellent forecast accuracy, hence offering efficient technological assistance for the functioning of the power system.

title: Small Dipole Modeling of Superdirective CSRR Loaded Dipole
authors: Monica Wasfy William, Yahia M. M. Antar, Alois Freundorfer
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Antenna Measurements and Applications (CAMA)
abstract: This paper presents an optimization approach of a superdirective complementary split ring resonator loaded dipole for millimeter wave applications. By inspecting main features of the current distribution, an equivalent model using an array of small dipoles was developed and studied. Using the proposed model, the surface current distribution was optimized to enhance the dipole’s directivity exceeding theoretical directivity limits. The proposed concept in this paper facilitates a time efficient optimization process by granting deeper insight into the surface current distribution and defining constrains to manipulate the radiation performance.

title: Bilingual Medical Llm for English-Tamil Healthcare Assistance Focusing on Ent Domain
authors: Jagathsri Santhanalakshmi A, Aneruthan Subbiah K, Pallakonda Honey, Ansuman Mahapatra, Jayasrilakshmi S
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Innovative Practices in Technology and Management (ICIPTM)
abstract: Bilingual medical question answering is essential for improving healthcare accessibility. It is especially important in regions where low-resource languages limit the effectiveness of existing AI systems. This work focuses on ENT-specific medical assistance by developing an English-Tamil bilingual language model capable of generating contextually accurate responses. A domain-focused dataset was constructed by filtering ENTrelated question-answer pairs from publicly available English medical sources and translating selected samples into Tamil using an LLM-based translation pipeline with manual refinement. The Tamil-LLaMA-7B-Instruct model was fine-tuned using the QLoRA framework in 4-bit precision to enable efficient adaptation on limited hardware. Model performance was assessed through GPT-4 based scoring across a set of ENT queries, supported by qualitative evaluation of response quality. The finetuned model exhibits notable improvements in Tamil fluency, instruction following, and ENT domain relevance compared to the base model. The proposed methodology demonstrates a practical direction for developing lightweight, bilingual medical assistants tailored to low-resource linguistic environments.

title: Fall-Prevention Power-Assisted Climbing Robot with Force-Position Adaptive Control
authors: Yingdong Rao, Lijuan Guo, Jing Mo, Peng Zhou, Guoshan Xie
publish: 2025 8th World Conference on Mechanical Engineering and Intelligent Manufacturing (WCMEIM)
abstract: This paper addresses the issues of high labor intensity and safety risks in manual climbing during power inspection operations, and designs a power-assisted climbing robot system with force-position adaptive control. By integrating hardware circuit design and dynamic model analysis, a rigid-flexible coupling control strategy based on redundant power drive is proposed. The system uses an STC15W4K32S4 single-chip microcontroller as the core controller, combined with tilt sensors and current sampling technology, to achieve coordinated adjustment of speed and load force during climbing. Mechanical analysis shows that through eccentric angle adjustment and PID algorithm optimization, the robot can achieve <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15\%-30\%$</tex> adaptive adjustment of lifting force in a T-shaped track environment. Experiments verify the stability and reliability of the system under different load conditions.

title: Analysis and Improvement of Open-Loop Current Detection Circuit
authors: Yuming Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: Aiming at the gain nonlinearity problem of openloop current sampling technology in high-frequency DC-DC converters, this paper proposes a new type of open-loop current detection circuit. In the traditional scheme, the gain nonlinearity is caused by the square root relationship between the transconductance and the sampling current. This scheme eliminates the transconductance mismatch caused by channel length modulation by introducing a diode-connected gain compensation output resistor and using cascode biasing to force the drain potentials of the input transistor and the transconductance ratio transistor to be equal. Simulation results show that when the sampling current varies in the range of 0 150 mA, the detection gain linearity of the proposed scheme is significantly better than that of the traditional scheme, achieving high-precision and fixed-gain sensing in a wide current range, which provides a reliable current detection solution for highfrequency current-mode controlled DC-DC converters.

title: Research on Multi-Channel Anomaly Detection Method for Photovoltaic Systems Based on Sliding Time Window and Dual-Model Fusion
authors: Jianfu Huang, Junqiang Zhuang, Chuzhi Huang, Weiliang Ouyang, Shaojie Zeng, Ruijin Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) systems often experience abnormal power output due to environmental variability and component aging, posing risks to system reliability. To enable precise monitoring and anomaly detection across multiple PV channels, this paper presents a hybrid framework combining Backpropagation (BP) neural networks with Transformer models. A sliding time window is adopted to dynamically generate training samples, improving sensitivity to non-stationary behavior. The dual-path architecture leverages BP's strength in short-term fluctuation modeling and the Transformer's ability to capture long-term dependencies, enabling multi-scale anomaly detection. To suppress false alarms, a relative-residual-based dynamic thresholding approach is introduced, along with a triple time-step consensus strategy to enhance detection stability. Experimental results confirm that the proposed method improves detection accuracy and reduces false positives, demonstrating strong potential for practical deployment.

title: Hybrid Quantum Neural Networks for Enhanced Breast Cancer Thermographic Classification: A Novel Quantum-Classical Integration Approach
authors: Riza Alaudin Syah, Irwan Alnarus Kautsar, Gunawan Witjaksono, Haza Nuzly bin Abdull Hamed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Biomedical Instrumentation and Technology Conference (IBITeC)
abstract: Breast cancer diagnosis through thermographic image analysis remains a critical challenge in medical AI, with classical deep learning approaches facing limitations in complex thermal pattern classification tasks. This paper presents a novel Hybrid Quantum Neural Network (HQNN) architecture that integrates quantum computing principles with classical convolutional neural networks for enhanced breast cancer classification. Our approach employs parameterized quantum circuits with multi-head attention mechanisms for quantum-aware feature encoding, coupled with classical convolutional layers for comprehensive pattern recognition. The quantum component utilizes a 4qubit variational circuit with strongly entangling layers, while the classical component incorporates advanced attention mechanisms for feature fusion. Experimental validation on breast cancer thermographic data demonstrates substantial performance improvements over state-of-the-art classical architectures, with the quantum-enhanced approach exhibiting superior convergence dynamics and enhanced feature representation capabilities. Our findings provide evidence for quantum advantage in medical image classification through classical simulation, establishing a framework for quantum-classical hybrid systems in healthcare applications. The methodology addresses key challenges in quantum machine learning deployment while maintaining computational feasibility on near-term quantum devices.

title: Finite Time and Robust $k$-Winners-Take-All Neural Network for Multiagents Coordination
authors: Jun Cai, Jinghan Liu, Lin Xiao, Chenfu Yi
publish: IEEE Systems Journal
abstract: In order to better adapt to the needs of practical scenarios, multiagents task allocation systems need to be able to select winners in the shortest possible time under external interference. Therefore, this article proposes a finite time and robust <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$k$</tex-math></inline-formula>-winners-take-all (FTR-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$k$</tex-math></inline-formula>WTA) neural network, by introducing a time-varying learning rate and a versatile activation function into the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$k$</tex-math></inline-formula>WTA neural network. Compared with existing <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$k$</tex-math></inline-formula>WTA neural networks, this network has faster finite-time convergence speed and noise suppression ability. The global convergence, finite-time convergence, and robustness of the proposed network have been demonstrated theoretically. Finally, these performances of the FTR-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$k$</tex-math></inline-formula>WTA neural<bold xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"/> network are further validated through simulation results. In addition, the multiagents system is simulated using E-puck robots on the CoppeliaSim platform, serving to confirm the efficacy of the FTR-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$k$</tex-math></inline-formula>WTA neural network.

title: CSAP-YOLOv8: A Cross-Scale Adaptive Perception Network for Ship Detection in Synthetic Aperture Radar Images
authors: Jiehong Wu, Congcong Wan, Lijun Gao, Cuiwei Liu, Chengcheng Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: With the increasing frequency of maritime activities, ship detection based on Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery plays a vital role in maritime surveillance and safety. However, in high-resolution SAR sea scenes, vessel targets typically exhibit characteristics such as small scale, slender structures, and dense distribution. Simultaneously, the influence of complex sea clutter and multi-scale variations poses significant challenges for existing detection methods in terms of feature representation consistency, cross-scale fusion, and precise localization.To address this, this paper proposes a Cross-Scale Adaptive Path YOLOv8 (CSAP-YOLOv8) vessel detection framework aimed at enhancing the robustness and generalization capabilities of SAR vessel detection in complex marine environments. Starting from the feature representation source, this method enhances the sustained characterization capability for small-scale and slender targets through cross-scale receptive field modeling. Simultaneously, it introduces an adaptive feature fusion mechanism to achieve semantic alignment and complementarity between features at different levels, mitigating semantic offset issues in multi-scale feature fusion. Furthermore, to address the instability of bounding box regression caused by SAR vessel geometry, a task-aware regression loss function is designed to improve localization accuracy through spatial position and shape constraints.Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a 3.5% improvement in AP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">50–95</sub> over baseline models on the HRSID dataset and yields even more significant performance gains on the RSDD-SAR dataset, validating its effectiveness and strong generalization capabilities across varying resolutions and complex scene conditions.

title: Hybrid TD3-PI Control for Wireless Charging of Light Electric Vehicles
authors: Akanksha, Afraz Ahmad, Ilamparithi Thirumarai Chelvan, Khanh Dat Truong
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This paper proposes a deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-assisted adaptive control strategy for wireless power transfer (WPT) in light electric vehicle (LEV) charging. A phase-shifted full-bridge converter with series–series compensation is considered, where the primary-side controller regulates the secondary charging current. Conventional proportional–integral (PI) control, tuned around nominal coupling, suffers performance degradation and even loss of convergence under coil misalignment and coupling variation. To address this, a Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) agent is used to adapt the PI gains online based on secondary-side feedback.The agent is trained in a Simulink environment and deployed in inference-only mode on a TI C2000 F28379D microcontroller, occupying approximately 25% of on-chip memory, demonstrating the feasibility of DRL-based adaptive control on resource-constrained embedded platforms. Experimental results show that the proposed TD3-assisted adaptive PI achieves constant-current regulation with significantly faster convergence compared to fixed-gain PI, settling time is reduced from 8.65 ms to 5.95 ms under aligned conditions, from 17.00 ms to 11.05 ms under lateral misalignment, and from 26.00 ms to 14.82 ms under angular misalignment, corresponding to improvements of about 35% and 43%. The faster transient response lowers stress and losses in the power switches. These results validate that DRL-enabled adaptive control enhances robustness of WPT-based LEV charging under realistic misalignment scenarios while remaining implementable on industrial-grade digital controllers.
